Question: What is Wi-Fi Calling?
Answer:
Wi-Fi Calling enables you to make and receive calls over a Wi-Fi network using your existing Jio number. The benefits of Wi-Fi calling in International Roaming are listed below: 
   
 Benefits of Wi-Fi calling in International Roaming:

 
 Make and receive voice calls on any Indian number with Wi-Fi using your existing Jio number at just ₹1/min 
 Video call your loved ones on any Jio number 
 Easily connect in case of signal issues in remote areas or dark cellular zones of buildings 
 Wi-Fi Calling doesn’t carry any additional charge. All you need is a Wi-Fi Calling pack and an HD voice-compatible device 
 To learn how to turn on Wi-Fi Calling and check the handset eligibility, click  www.jio.com/wificalling 
 
 
 Note: The charging for Wi-Fi Calling will be deducted from the respective IR pack balance. Charging for video calls will be deducted from the IR Pack balance and home data bucket. 
   
 A base plan with sufficient home data balance should be available to make video calls to the Jio number. Grace plan customers will not be able to use Jio Wi-Fi Calling services.

Question: How to enable Wi-Fi Calling?
Answer:
You can enable Wi-Fi Calling in three simple steps:

 
 
 Go to Settings and switch on the Wi-Fi Calling feature on your Wi-Fi Calling capable handset 
 Connect to a Wi-Fi network and enjoy Wi-Fi Calling on any Indian number 
 To learn how to turn on Wi-Fi Calling and check the handset eligibility, click  www.jio.com/wificalling 
 
 
 Note: Some applications can download data even when you are not actively using them. We recommend setting your ‘Mobile Data’ as ‘OFF’ or managing your application settings to avoid this data usage.

Question: What services are currently available on Wi-Fi Calling while roaming internationally?
Answer:
Wi-Fi Calling pack  offers the following benefits:
 
 Outgoing calls to any Indian mobile number 
 Incoming calls from India or anywhere in the world 
 Incoming SMS 
 Video calls to any Jio number in India 
 Local calls within the visited country or calls to the rest of the world are currently unavailable 
 Video calls on the non-Jio number and outgoing SMS are not allowed

Question: Does Wi-Fi Calling consume data? How much data will a Wi-Fi call consume?
Answer:
Here’s how  Wi-Fi Calling   works under Jio postpaid plans:
 
 Voice calls are consumed from your IR pack balance and do not consume data 
 Wi-Fi Calling for Video calls consumes data from the home data plan balance 
 The actual data usage of your video call may vary 
 Wi-Fi Calling for video calls is allowed only to Jio numbers in India

Question: Why am I unable to make or receive Wi-Fi calls on my Jio Postpaid SIM while abroad?
Answer:
If you are unable to use Wi-Fi calling on your Jio Postpaid number abroad, check the following: 
 Device Support: Ensure your phone supports Wi-Fi Calling. 
 Software: Update your device to the latest software version. 
 Settings: Go to your phone settings and switch ON "Wi-Fi Calling." 
 Internet: Ensure you are connected to a stable, high-speed Wi-Fi network. 
 Active Plan: Ensure you have an active International Roaming pack that supports Wi-Fi calling. 
 Still facing issues? 
 Global WhatsApp Support: Message "Hi" to +91 70188 99999. Our specialized International Roaming team is available 24/7 to help you get connected.

Question: Why am I facing call mute/call drop issues while using the Wi-Fi Calling service?
Answer: Wi-Fi Calling requires a stable Wi-Fi connection. You may experience a call drop issue if you are not within the Wi-Fi coverage area or if the Wi-Fi network quality is poor.

Question: Can I call someone who does not have Wi-Fi Calling enabled on his/her phone?
Answer: Yes, you will be able to make calls to any Indian number depending on your plan.

Question: Can I call on Emergency, Satellite and Special Services Numbers using Wi-Fi Calling Services?
Answer: No, Wi-Fi Calling does not allow calls to Emergency, Satellite or Special services numbers.

Question: Will Wi-Fi Calling work on a prepaid card of Jammu & Kashmir, Assam & North East when in International Roaming?
Answer: As per Indian Regulations, IR pack purchases for Jammu & Kashmir, Assam and North East are not allowed on prepaid subscriptions, due to which you will not be able to use  Wi-Fi Calling  .

Question: Can I use Wi-Fi Calling while in Airplane mode?
Answer: Yes, the  Wi-Fi Calling  service can be used when your phone is in ‘Airplane Mode'.

Question: Do I have to activate IR service to be able to use In-Flight services?
Answer: No. You only have to recharge with the In-Flight pack to be able to use In-Flight Data, Voice and text services. Your  IR service   will get activated on recharge of the In-Flight Pack automatically.

Question: I am an In-Flight Pack user, will I be charged for incoming messages while on In-Flight?
Answer: All incoming text messages are free for In-Flight pack users. For non-pack users In-Flight services are not available and hence, incoming messages cannot be received.

Question: What happens if my In-Flight Pack gets expired?
Answer:
In-Flight Pack offers limited entitlements of Data, Voice and text usage. 
   
 
 You may choose suitable In-Flight pack as per your In-Flight usage requirements 
 You can also do multiple recharges of In-Flight Pack 
 You can do another recharge of In-Flight pack through  MyJio  or  jio.com  before expiry of your existing IFC pack and exhaustion of data entitlement 
 
   
 Please note that In-flight services are available only to In-Flight pack users as long as validity and entitlements are available. In-flight services are not available on PayGo rates.

Question: Will I be charged for incoming calls while using In-flight service?
Answer: Incoming calls are not allowed in Jio Inflight service. Only Inflight Data, Outgoing voice and SMS services are available, to the active In-Flight Pack users.

Question: I have recharged with an In-Flight Pack. Which all outgoing voice calls I can make?
Answer: All voice outgoing calls (except calls to satellite, premium IR numbers, Short codes, and special numbers) are allowed.

Question: I have recharged with an In-Flight Pack, when will the pack validity start?
Answer:
Your pack will remain in queue until you make a call/data/SMS while in the flight. On the first usage instance on the In-Flight Pack, the pack validity starts and remains valid for next 24 hours from that time. 
   
 
 Please note In-flight services network will be available only in the covered flight and when your flight attains height of 20,000 feet above the ground.

Answer:
Jio Inflight service allows you to stay connected in the covered flight at above 20000 feet. You need to recharge with Jio prepaid in-flight communication Packs before onboarding the flight. 
   
 Customers with active In-flight connectivity (IFC) Packs, will be able to use data, outgoing voice calls and SMS (wherever applicable) as per the pack entitlement quota available.

Question: How can I use Jio IFC Packs and the steps activate IFC Packs?
Answer:
To use IFC Packs providing inflight data, outgoing voice calls and SMS,  you have to recharge with the IFC Packs (applicable for both prepaid and postpaid customers) before boarding the flight  when connected to Jio network. 
   
 Once you are in the flight and the flight has reached 20,000 feet and above, follow these steps:

 
 Switch on your phone with Airplane Mode off 
 Your phone will automatically connect to the AeroMobile network. The network name may differ dependent on the handset 
 If your phone does not connect to the AeroMobile network automatically you will need to go to ‘Carrier’ in your phone settings and manually select AeroMobile 
 Ensure Data Roaming is on to use data services 
 Once connected, you will receive a welcome text and pricing information 
 You can now use your mobile phone to call, text, email and surf the internet* 
 
 
 Incoming calls are not allowed. You will need to disable Wi-Fi connectivity to use mobile data. As a courtesy to fellow passengers, please keep your phone on vibrate. 
   
 *Please note - some applications can download data even when you are not actively using them, and you may be charged as a result. Manage your application settings to help avoid these costs. International roaming rates apply.

Question: Can I recharge with the IFC Packs after reaching another country?
Answer: Yes, you can recharge with the In-flight pack on  jio.com /  MyJio  by connecting to either a mobile or Wi-Fi network, just before boarding your flight.

Question: What is the Data speed I will get in the IFC Packs?
Answer: The network speed of In-Flight connectivity is dependent on the in-flight network operators (OnAir or AeroMobile) and the regulations governing air travel. The in-flight network operators are constantly reviewing and innovating their services to provide a better surfing experience for the customers.

Question: Can I use IFC Packs while the plane is in Indian Airspace?
Answer: No, as per regulation In-Flight Communication (IFC) services are currently not available while you are in Indian Air Space.

Question: How can I activate In-Flight Pack?
Answer:
To use In-Flight pack providing in-flight data, outgoing voice calls and SMS, you have to recharge with the IFC Packs before boarding the flight. 

Once the flight has reached 20,000 feet, please follow these steps:

 
 Switch ON your phone with Airplane Mode Off 
 Your phone will automatically connect to the AeroMobile network (the network name may differ dependent on the handset) 
 If your phone does not connect to the AeroMobile network automatically, you will need to go to ‘Carrier’ in your phone settings and manually select ‘AeroMobile’ 
 Ensure Data Roaming is ON to use data services 
 
 Incoming calls are not allowed. You will need to disable Wi-Fi connectivity to use mobile data. As a courtesy to fellow passengers, please keep your phone on vibrate.

Question: What if I am unable to use data services In-flight?
Answer:
If your phone does not connect to the AeroMobile network automatically you will need to go to ‘Carrier’ in your phone settings and manually select AeroMobile. 
   
 Step 1: To use data services, you will need to ensure Data Roaming is On 

Step 2: Once connected, you will receive a welcome text and pack benefits information 
 Step 3: You can now use your mobile phone to call, text, email and surf the internet* 
   
 *Please note that some applications can download data even when you are not actively using them, and you may be charged as a result. Manage your application settings to help avoid these costs.

Question: How do I make calls when in flight, and having IFC Packs activated?
Answer: Dial '+' followed by the country code, then key in the phone number. For example, to call from Singapore to India, dial +917018899999

Question: Can I access Jio's media apps while in flight?
Answer: Jio media apps ( JioTV , etc.) are designed to work only within India. They are not available on international roaming.

Question: Will prepaid card of J&K, Assam & North East work when in In-Flight International Roaming under Postpaid Plans?
Answer: No. In-Flight service is an International Roaming service. As per prevailing Indian Regulations, Prepaid subscribers of J&K, Assam & North East will not be allowed to use International Roaming service.

Question: I have subscribed to IFC Packs. Will I be able to make Video calls while in the flight?
Answer:
No, video calling feature is currently not available in Inflight services. 
 However, if you are using any OTT app (like WhatsApp) to make the video call then you will be able to continue to do so provided you have kept the data roaming ON. The data will be consumed from your IFC Pack entitlements.

Question: How do I get  support for In-Flight Roaming  or network issues while I am in the flight?

If you are connected to the In-Flight network and require support:

 Voice Calls: Minutes will be deducted from your active In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) Pack voice entitlement.

Question: How do I check my IFC pack Data, voice and SMS balance entitlements?
Answer:
You can check your usage details on  MyJio  or  jio.com . Login into your account, click the 'Usage' tab to check your usage details. 
 Note that your usage details are not real time and will have to check after few hours for recent data usage details.

Question: What happens when Aircraft is closer to the ground?
Answer:
During take-off or landing, if you have recharged with a normal international roaming pack (₹1101 with unlimited data international roaming pack, etc.) and you are using the internet, the pack may be automatically activated due to low elevation of the aircraft, and strong signals from ground levels. 
   
 To prevent this from happening, you may wish to turn on flight-mode on your phone during take-off and landing, and when the aircraft is near your destination.

Question: Are there any subscription or rentals charges for International Roaming (IR) service for Jio Prepaid Plans?
Answer: No, there are no subscription or monthly rentals for IR service.

Question: If I recharge with a Voice & SMS-only domestic plan (e.g., ₹448 , ₹1748), can I use it for International Roaming?

To use International Roaming, you will need to  recharge  with an additional International Roaming (IR) pack of your choice.  Jio 5G plans starting 349 and above.

Question: What are the terms for free JioHotstar subscription?
Answer:
Customer must stay active in eligible plans to be eligible to get benefits for full duration of the offer. 
 
 User recharging with prepaid plans with validity upto 28 days/1 month/30 Days, 1-month JioHotstar will be activated. User needs to recharge within 48 hours of their existing plan expiry to get Hotstar benefit in the 2nd and 3rd month 
 If recharged with prepaid plans with validity between 29/31Days - 56 days, 2 months of JioHotstar will be activated. User needs to recharge within 48 hours of their existing plan expiry to get Hotstar benefit in 3rd month 
 On recharge with prepaid plans with validity more than 56 days, 3 months of JioHotstar will be activated. 
 
 If not recharged within the time limit, subsequent JioHotstar subscriptions will not be credited.

Question: How can I get Unlimited 5G data offer on my Jio SIM?

If you're looking to unlock 5G unlimited data with zero hassle, Jio makes it effortless. Since Jio runs on a true 5G-ready network, you don’t need a new SIM to enjoy 5G SIM unlimited data or unlimited 5G speeds. Your existing SIM is already future-ready. 
   
 If you’re on a 2GB/day or above plan, you automatically get unlimited 5G data. 
 But even if you’re on a lower daily data plan, you can upgrade yourself to unlimited data 5G at any time by choosing an add-on and instantly activating Jio True 5G unlimited benefits. 
   
 If you use a 1.5GB/day plan 
   
 
 ₹51 Add-on: If your plan validity is up to 1 month, recharge to get unlimited 5G data. 
 ₹101 Add-on: If your plan validity is >1 month & ≤2 months, recharge to enjoy 5G network unlimited data. 
 ₹151 Add-on: If your plan validity is >2 months & ≤3 months, recharge to activate 5G internet unlimited benefits. 
 
   
 If you use a 1GB/day plan 
   
 
 ₹101 Add-on: Recharge to unlock 5G phone unlimited data instantly. 
 
 Note: Your unlimited 5G benefits stay active for the duration of your current base plan.

Question: What are the benefits of purchasing pre-owned smartphones under the Upgrade Offer?
Answer: The Upgrade Offer, powered by Jio, entitles all Jio customers to buy select pre-owned smartphones and avail themselves of savings worth up to ₹1,500 on recharges.

Question: How do I avail myself of the benefits under the Upgrade Offer?
Answer:
You can avail of the benefits under the Upgrade Offer by following the simple steps below: 
 
 Insert your Jio SIM into the pre-owned smartphone and enable data services on Jio 
 Download the MyJio app and log in with your Jio number 
 Recharge with ₹239 or above ((except value recharges ₹395, ₹1559 & ₹2545 ) 
 Additional data benefits of 0.5 GB/day will be transferred through the MyJio app

	 
 Additional data vouchers will be visible under the Vouchers section 
 Additional data vouchers will have to be redeemed by the user 
 Additional data vouchers will be valid for the duration of the base prepaid plan

Question: If I switch to another Jio number, will my pre-owned smartphone under the Upgrade Offer continue to work smoothly?
Answer: Yes, all you need is an active Jio SIM with a valid recharge plan. Please note that you can avail of the Upgrade Offer benefits only once against one Jio SIM.

Question: Will I be able to use another operator's SIM on a pre-owned smartphone bought under the Upgrade Offer?

Question: What if I want to use data on another operator's network on my pre-owned smartphone under the Upgrade Offer?
Answer: For pre-owned smartphones under the Upgrade Offer, you will be able to access data only on the Jio network.

Question: What should I do if I am unable to make calls or use data on my pre-owned smartphone under the Upgrade Offer?
Answer: If you are unable to make calls or use data on your pre-owned smartphone under the Upgrade Offer, please contact our customer helpline number at 198 or 1800-889-9999

Question: Can I use data over Wi-Fi on the pre-owned smartphone bought under the Upgrade Offer?
Answer: Yes, you can access data over Wi-Fi on a pre-owned smartphone under the Upgrade Offer only if you have a Jio SIM with a valid recharge plan in the smartphone.

Question: What if my Jio SIM doesn t have a valid recharge plan on the pre-owned smartphone under the Upgrade Offer?
Answer: You will receive recharge reminders from Jio to recharge your Jio SIM on time. If your Jio SIM does not have a valid recharge, your smartphone will only be able to make emergency calls and customer care calls. You will be able to use the full features of your smartphone once you perform a valid recharge on your Jio SIM.

Question: What if I don t use a Jio SIM with a valid recharge plan on my pre-owned smartphone under the Upgrade Offer?
Answer: If your Jio SIM does not have a valid recharge, your pre-owned smartphone screen will be restricted. You will only be able to make emergency calls, call customer care, and access the MyJio app to recharge your Jio number.

Question: What should I keep in mind while using the pre-owned smartphone under the Upgrade Offer?
Answer: You need to insert a Jio SIM with a valid recharge plan and ensure data services are enabled on the Jio SIM. In case you do not have an active Jio plan, just recharge with an applicable Jio smartphone prepaid plan.

Question: What are the eligible recharges under the Upgrade Offer?
Answer: You can recharge with any Jio smartphone prepaid plan of ₹239 and above, except plans such as ₹395, ₹1,559 and ₹2,545.

Question: Can I perform a factory reset on the pre-owned smartphone?
Answer: No, you cannot perform a factory reset on the smartphone as the device is restricted.

Question: Can I exit the Upgrade Offer by unlocking my pre-owned smartphone?
Answer: Yes, you can exit the Upgrade Offer by unlocking the smartphone within 90 days of locking it without any penalty charges. If the device has been locked for more than 90 days, you have to pay ₹300 administrative charges..

Question: How many pre-owned smartphones can I buy under the Upgrade Offer?
Answer: You can buy as many smartphones as you want. However, you can avail of the Upgrade Offer benefits only once against one Jio SIM under the Upgrade Offer..

Question: How can I unlock my pre-owned smartphone under the Upgrade Offer?
Answer: You can unlock a pre-owned smartphone by going to the MyJio app, tapping on ‘Settings’ and selecting ‘Unlock – Upgrade by Jio’. Enter IMEI (International Mobile Equipment Identity) to validate and proceed to unlock your pre-owned smartphone

Question: Who can avail of the benefits under the Upgrade Offer?
Answer: Any Jio customer with a valid recharge plan can avail of the benefits of the Upgrade Offer. Non-Jio customers can avail of these benefits by activating a new Jio SIM or migrating their existing number to Jio through MNP.

Question: Can I change my Jio number and continue to avail the benefits?
Answer: Yes, the benefits of the Upgrade Offer are linked to the pre-owned smartphone. The cashback voucher can be availed of on any Jio number being used on that pre-owned smartphone via the MyJio app.

Question: What is "Genpact Employee's Friends and Family" Offer?
Answer:
Jio has introduced special benefits for Friends and Family members of Genpact employees. 

To avail this offer you need to be on  Postpaid Plan  199 and enrol using MyJio App. 

Once successfully enrolled, you will get 20% more data on Postpaid Plan 199 every month in form of 5 GB additional data voucher. 

Also, you will get one time enrolment benefits of Rs 250 (Rs 50 X 5) in form of cashback vouchers and one AJIO discount coupon worth Rs.399

Question: How to opt for "Genpact Employee's Friends and Family" Offer?
Answer:
To enrol for this offer, you need to be on  Postpaid Plan  199. 

Step 1: Open MyJio app and Go to menu option and select "Offers" 

Step 2: Select "Genpact Employee Offer" and Enter the Corporate email Id of your known friend or family member working with Genpact. 

Step 3: Your relative / friend (Genpact Employee) will receive an OTP on his/her corporate email id. 

Step 4: Please ask for that OTP number from your relative / friend (Genpact Employee) and enter the same in MyJio App 

Step 5: Post submitting the OTP, you will see a confirmation message on MyJio App 

Post that you will receive the following benefits in your account: 

1) 5GB Additional data voucher which will be credited every month on the same date. 

2) One time benefit of Rs 250 as cashback vouchers (Rs 50 Cashback voucher X 5), which can be redeemed against Pre on Post Recharges of Plan MRP 198 and 299 only. 

3) One AJIO discount coupon worth 399 which can be redeemed against a minimum transaction value of Rs. 1000. This coupon is valid till 31st March 2019.

The behaviour on Jio network, of L7 smartphone will be as follows: 
   
 
 
 
 
 Scenario 
 
 
 Device Behavior 
 
 
 
 
 SIM 1 – Jio 
 SIM 2 – Other Operator

Question: What is JioGames Cloud?
Answer: JioGames  Cloud is a cutting-edge cloud gaming platform that allows anyone with a smartphone or a regular laptop/desktop or Jio-STB to play high-end console-quality games without needing any additional hardware.

Question: Does JioGames Cloud have a multi-platform play?
Answer: Yes,  JioGames  Cloud supports multi-platform play. Users can play on different platforms using a single account that has an active subscription. Progress saved by a game on one platform, can be continued on another supported platform of the user’s choice. However, it's important to note that a specific user can only be active on one platform at a time. The user will not be able to play on multiple platforms simultaneously.

Question: What do I need to play games?
Answer: To play on  JioGames  Cloud, you would need either a Smartphone, or a Laptop/Desktop or a Jio-STB with a stable Internet Connection and an active JioGames Cloud gaming plan.

Question: Do I need to use the Jio Controller to play games on Jio STB?
Answer: JioGames  Cloud can be best experienced with a JioGames Controller. However, many aftermarket controllers are compatible with JioGames Cloud, allowing you to use these controllers to play games on Jio STB. List of some compatible controllers can be found at https://jiogames.com/supported-controllers .

Question: What are the plan options available in JioGames Cloud bundle?
Answer: You can opt for  JioGames  Cloud bundled connectivity plans or JioGames Cloud add-ons along with your Jio mobile connection. You will find multiple options of plans and addon combinations at www.Jio.com, MyJio application, and other recharge platforms.

Question: How many users are allowed access per account?

Answer: Please check whether you have latest version of  MyJio  App, if not, update the App. If you have the latest App & banner is still not visible, you should reach out to Jio customer support at  care@jio.com

Question: What is the process if I have forgotten my Amazon Login ID?
Answer: You contact Jio customer support and receive the External ID. You can then share your External ID with Amazon customer support to get help with login credentials. Amazon customer support gets the required information and contacts back to you to recover login information.

Question: I have upgraded to an eligible JioPostpaid Plus plan but I am still not able to watch Prime Video?
Answer: You need to activate Amazon Prime through  MyJio  app to get one year of Amazon Prime at no extra cost. Also, please note that eligible  JioPostPaid  Plus plan need to be active plan and only after that you will be able to activate Amazon Prime through MyJio App.

Question: In case where my JioPostpaid Plus connection is in suspension/termination stage and I pay/clear my dues, will I get Amazon Prime benefit again?
Answer: 1 year Amazon Prime subscription offer is a one time offer. In case the offer is cancelled, you will not be able to enjoy the same benefits even after clearing past dues and reactivating the connection.

Question: What is JioAutoPay for Prepaid?
Answer: JioAutoPay  is a facility by which you can opt for recharge of your Prepaid number by using bank account number authenticating via net banking or debit card.

Question: From where can I opt for JioAutoPay?
Answer: You can set up  JioAutoPay  through Self-care (Jio.com & MyJio app). Shortly it shall be also available at Jio Stores/Retailers.

Question: When will my card / account / wallet be charged if I have set up a JioAutoPay for recharge?
Answer:
Payment for  JioAutoPay  shall be presented to the bank 5 days before the date of expiry of current plan. However for long-term plans with no daily FUP, 
 JioAutoPay shall be presented to the bank basis your data usage reaching 90% of the data entitlement of your plan. Please note that Jio reserves the right to make changes to the above clause threshold based on the data usage and payment cycles. Jio will inform you about such change in advance.

Question: When will I know if my account is getting debited?
Answer: You will be notified 7 days before the expiry of the plan regarding the auto recharge debit. 5 days before the plan expiry,  JioAutoPay  will be presented to the bank for payment and re-attempted before 1 day in case of first failed attempt. This will be notified on your registered email account & registered mobile number.

Question: Can I set up JioAutoPay for Add-on / Top Up / ISD / roaming packs?
Answer: No you cannot set up the  JioAutoPay  for Add-on / Top Up / ISD  roaming packs. The JioAutoPay is applicable only on  recharge  plan.

Question: If due to any reason, the recharge through JioAutoPay fails, will I be notified?
Answer: Yes, you will be notified through your registered email account & registered mobile number with the recharge failure reasons.

Question: If due to any reason, the recharge through JioAutoPay fails, will Jio reattempt the transaction automatically or will I have to recharge again?
Answer: If one or more successive payments/instructions are not honored on or before the due date, Jio reserves the right to withdraw the  JioAutoPay  facility without giving any notice, and to initiate any other action/proceedings as may be deemed appropriate by the Jio for recovery of the unpaid amount from you. In the event your standing instruction is dishonored by bank, penalty charge (or such other amount as specified by Jio from time to time) may be levied per instance of such failure of receipt of payment.

Question: I already have 3 plans in queue and I have signed up for JioAutoPay. Will the charging start automatically based on current plan expiry OR after expiry of all plans in queue?
Answer: After the expiry of all the queued plans, the  JioAutoPay  services will be activated however, only >= 149 paid queue plan shall be considered as queued plan for JioAutoPay instruction to bank.

Question: Will I receive an invoice in case of JioAutoPay debits?
Answer: Yes. An invoice will be available to you through Self-Care (MyJio/Jio.com).

Question: How can I change the recharge amount in JioAutoPay?
Answer:
The option to change the  JioAutoPay  Recharge Amount is available on the  MyJio app  and www.jio.com/myaccount. 
   
 Here're steps to change your plan via MyJio:
 
 Step 1: Open the MyJio app 
 Step 2: Click on the menu option (top left corner) 
 Step 3: Tap on 'JioPay' 
 Step 4: Select  JioAutoPay 
 Step 5: Under details, select 'Change Plan' 
 Step 6: Select the “Plan” of your choice and click on proceed 
 Step 7: You will see a confirmation message and an SMS notification stating: 
 
 “Your request for 'Change Plan' has been successfully submitted. 
 
Any modification/cancellation of AutoPay shall be effective from the upcoming debit trigger of AutoPay which is 5 days before the expiry of the (last) active plan or upon 90% of the data consumption for long-term plans with no daily data limit."

Question: How can I cancel JioAutoPay?
Answer:
Please find below steps to cancel JioAutoPay through  MyJio  App. 
Step 1 : Open MyJio App 
Step 2 : Tap on the Menu option (Top Left corner) 
Step 3 : Tap on JioPay and Select  JioAutoPay 
Step 4 : Now select the Bank/Credit Card no. details, click on "Cancel" to cancel JioAutoPay

Question: What is the UPI tab in MyJio? What is it used for? (OR) What kind of transactions can I perform using UPI?

You can perform various transactions using a Jio UPI handle like instant money transfer from 1 bank to another, Bill Payment,  Recharge  and so also pay merchants who accept UPI as payment mode. The UPI tab is currently available to all MyJio Customers.

Question: What billers are available for use for UPI?
Answer: All billers which are under the Bharat  BillPay System are available for use under  MyJio  UPI.

Question: I don t have a Jio number, will I be able to create a Jio UPI handle?
Answer: You can create an @Jio UPI handle only if you are a   MyJio or JioMoney customer.

Question: Can I use my existing UPI ID in MyJio?
Answer: To use the UPI service from  MyJio app  , you will have to create a unique Jio handle by linking your Savings, Current or Overdraft Account to it. Any other existing UPI ID cannot be used

Question: How many Bank accounts can I link with MyJio?
Answer:
There is no limit on the number of bank accounts that you can link with  MyJio . 
However please assure that the bank account that you are trying to link to MyJio should be linked with the mobile number that was used while creating the UPI Account. As UPI fetches the bank account from the mobile number that was used while creating the UPI account.

Question: Is MyJio UPI safe to use?
Answer: MyJio  UPI service is highly secure platform where user data safety is our utmost priority. We follow all the necessary guidelines and have undertaken all the measures to make this digital payment platform safer for you.

Question: How do I create a UPI Handle on MyJio?
Answer: When you first complete the device binding process for  MyJio  UPI, you will find your VPA handle already created for you at the end of the process. This handle will always be your PhoneNumber@Jio.

Question: What is the difference between VPA and UPI?
Answer:
VPA or Handle  – A VPA or a Handle is a unique identifier for your account which allows us to identify your transactions as exclusively yours. 
 UPI  – UPI is the service that creates these handles and allows you to make payments.

Question: How many UPI/VPA handles can be added to my number?
Answer:
You can add only 3 VPA handle to your number. 

Once the VPA handle is added, even if you delete any one of the VPA handle that you have added before, you will not be able to add anymore VPA handle further.

Question: How many times can I attempt device binding in a day?
Answer:
You can make 3 attempts of Device binding in a day. 
Once the limit is breached you will not be able to proceed from the  SIM  selection screen and will have to wait for next 24 hours to retry device binding.

Question: Why has my device binding request declined?
Answer: If the same SMS of device binding is copied from the original source (device) and sent to another number on a different device, then device binding request will be declined for both the numbers.

Question: Why is my UPI handle not visible?
Answer: Your UPI Handle will be visible only for the latest version of  MyJio  that is 6.0.06 and above. Please update your MyJio version from app store or play store.

Question: Why has my device binding request failed?
Answer:
You must complete your device binding process within 30 seconds from the time you have initiated. 

However, if you have exceeded the time limit your request will fail and you will have to try again..

Question: Why is the UPI tab not visible in MyJio App of my iOS device (For iPhone customers only)?
Answer: You will be able to view the UPI tab in  MyJio  App only if your iOS version is above 6 and 6s.

Question: Why did my verification SMS fail for my iOS device? (For iPhone customers only)
Answer:
You must send the verification SMS within 5 seconds from the time of process initiation. 

However, if you exceed the time limit your SMS verification request will fail and you will have to send SMS again.

Question: What is UPI PIN?
Answer:
The UPI PIN is a 4 or 6-digit pin that you can create during your first time registration with  MyJio  UPI. 
This PIN is unique to your bank account and not to your mobile number or UPI handle. 
The length of the PIN is bank specific and is not decided by MyJio. 
UPI PIN is very confidential as it is used to authenticate all your UPI transactions, hence do not share it with anyone.

Question: How can I reset my UPI PIN? What happens if I forgot my UPI PIN?
Answer: As your UPI PIN is unique to your bank account and not to your mobile number, you can reset it when you register a bank account or by choosing “Reset Pin “option for the bank account in the  MyJio  App

Question: I am putting correct UPI PIN however it is not being accepted, what do I do?
Answer: Please reset your UPI Pin for the account by choosing “Reset Pin” option for the bank account.

Question: What will happen if I enter the wrong UPI-PIN during a transaction?
Answer: If you enter the wrong PIN, your transaction will be declined. You will have to retry the transaction with correct UPI PIN. Please reset your UPI PIN by choosing “Reset Pin “option for the bank account if you have forgotten your UPI PIN.

Question: How can I pay an online merchant through UPI?
Answer:
You can pay a merchant by inputting your  MyJio  UPI handle at the merchant website, and then accepting the request that is sent to your MyJio UPI account. 
You may also pay the merchant by scanning a QR code presented by the merchant.

Question: Why is the QR code reader not working/scanning, while performing Physical Merchant transaction?
Answer: QR codes which are not compliant with the latest standards for QR codes, they may not work. Please try performing at other Merchant Outlets.

Question: Do I need to register before performing a UPI transaction?
Answer: Yes, you would be required to register for UPI services on  MyJio  before you can perform a transaction.

Question: Do I need to register a beneficiary before transferring funds through UPI?
Answer:
For UPI transactions you are not required to register a beneficiary for fund transfer. 

You can send money by QR code, mobile number or bank account with IFSC code combination and a UPI handle.

Question: Can my UPI handle be linked to a card or wallet?
Answer: UPI is directly linked with bank account. A credit/debit card or a wallet is not required for UPI transaction.

Question: What is the Jio Matching number?
Answer:
Jio Matching Number  allows you to choose a new Jio mobile number that is similar to your existing Jio or non-Jio number. It is ideal for twinning with a partner, gifting to a friend, or using matching numbers within a family.   
 You can browse and select matching number options online at  https://www.jio.com/  or visit a Jio retail store to buy a mobile number of your choice.   
 The service suggests numbers with similar matching digits based on your current number. This makes it easy to select a number that feels familiar and personalised.   
 Matching numbers are available for both prepaid and postpaid connections, subject to availability and the selected plan.   
 Special Offer:  Get a matching number worth ₹499 for just ₹50.   
 ₹349 Hybrid Prepaid Eligibility:  The matching number can be activated with the ₹349 hybrid plan, which includes:

 - Unlimited 5G data

Question: How does a Jio Matching Number work?
Answer:
Jio Matching Number  helps you own a number similar to your current one. To book a matching number, you have to go through a step where you choose your new number from all the available similar numbers.   
 Here’s how it works:   
 - When you go to the matching number link, you need to enter your current mobile number, and in case a non-Jio number, we need your PIN code to check the availability of matching numbers in your area.   
 - We’ll show you matching mobile number options based on patterns like repeats or sequences. Pick the one you like - your chosen number will be reserved just for you.   
 - Quickly book the number. As thousands of matching numbers are picked every day, we’re not sure if it will be available the next time you try.   
 - After that, your SIM will be delivered and activated through our standard SIM activation process. You can also choose to activate your number via self-KYC by completing the KYC process online.

Question: Why choose a Jio Matching Number?
Answer:
A  Jio Matching Number  makes it easy to personalise your mobile connection and build a shared identity with your loved ones. Whether its family members, couples, or close friends, matching numbers help you stay connected with a sense of familiarity and belonging.  
    
 With a matching number, you can:  
 - Create similar or sequential mobile numbers for your family or group  
 - Choose a number that you feel matches most and therefore would be easy to remember  
 - Enjoy better recall, making your number easier to remember and share  
 - Add a personal touch to your Jio connection  
 - Strengthen your family or group identity with numbers that feel connected  
    
 A matching number is perfect for families, couples, and anyone who values simplicity, convenience, and personalisation in their mobile experience.

Question: What happens to the plan after the initial 28 days?
Answer: After the 28-day validity period, you can recharge with any available Jio prepaid plan to continue enjoying seamless connectivity. Explore and select plans via www.jio.com or the  MyJio  app.

Question: Can I switch to a postpaid plan with my matching number?
Answer: Yes, one of the key features of  Jio Matching number  is that you can switch to any available Jio postpaid plan through  www.jio.com  or the  MyJio  app. During the plan change, you’ll need to make the applicable payment as per Jio’s standard process.

Question: Can I get a matching number for a friend or family member?
Answer: Absolutely! You can book a  matching number  for friends or family. Simply share the booked number and booking code with them to complete the activation process.

Question: How do I select a Jio Matching number?
Answer:
A Jio Matching Number is a mobile number that is similar to your existing number. You need to provide your existing number. In case you have entered a non-Jio number, we would also need your PIN Code to check the availability of similar, matching numbers in your area.   
 The existing number can be any number - either yours or your loved ones with whom you want to match your new mobile number.   
 Just keep in mind, the number has to be handy so that you can provide the OTP for verification and proceed to order it.   
  For more details please visit  https://www.jio.com/selfcare/choice-number/

Question: Will I receive a monthly bill after activating my Matching Number?
Answer: No, you won't receive any monthly bill. With your  Matching Number  you only need to recharge to continue using the service. A monthly bill will only be generated if you move to a Postpaid billing plan

Question: Can I get a Matching Number on a Prepaid plan instead of Postpaid?

Question: What does a "Hybrid Prepaid Plan" mean?
Answer:
Hybrid Plans are available for recharge by both Prepaid and Postpaid users, no matter what type of connection you have. 
 Even though your  Matching Number  is a Postpaid connection, it behaves just like a Prepaid connection,  giving you the flexibility to recharge, with no monthly bills.

Question: Why I am not able to generate UPC code in Jio Family Matching Number?

Question: What is VoNR/Vo5G? What is the benefit of VoNR/Vo5G?
Answer:
VoNR (Voice over New Radio) is a way to make phone calls using a 5G network, like how VoLTE (Voice over LTE) works on 4G networks. You just need to be in a 5G coverage area, and your device must support VoNR. Also, ensure that your mobile is updated with the latest software released by your device manufacturer, and voice over 5G/NR settings are enabled. 
 With VoNR-enabled, you can enjoy: 
 
 Clearer voice calls with better sound quality.  
 Quicker connections when making calls. i.e. your calls will connect faster, so you spend less time waiting for the call to go through  
 Use 5G data and calls at the same time without interruptions. This means you can browse the internet, stream videos, or use apps that require data while you’re on a call, without experiencing call drops or speed issues.

Question: VoNR/Vo5G is visible on my device, what is it?
Answer: If VoNR is visible on your device, it means your phone can make voice calls over a 5G network (Voice over New Radio, VoNR). VoNR , i.e. Voice over New Radio, is a way to make phone calls using a 5G network, like how VoLTE (Voice over LTE) works on 4G networks. You just need to be in a 5G coverage area, and your device must support VoNR. Ensure that your mobile is updated with latest Software released by your device manufacturer & Voice over 5G/NR settings is enabled.

Question: How do I check if my device supports VoNR/Vo5G?
Answer: You can check your device's compatibility by searching for 'VoNR/Vo5G/Voice over 5G SA' in 'Settings' under 'Mobile Networks', or you can confirm the same by visiting the manufacturer's website or at their service centre.

Question: Is VoNR/Vo5G available in my area?
Answer: The VoNR feature is rolled out across pan-India. If you are a 5G user with a 5G device, you can make voice calls using VoNR. Just keep the VoNR 'ON' from your network/data settings and start using. However, you need to ensure that your mobile is updated with the latest software released by your device manufacturer, and voice over 5G/NR settings are enabled.

Question: Why is VoNR/Vo5G not working even though I am connected to a 5G network?
Answer: Ensure your phone supports VoNR/Vo5G and is updated to the latest software version, and voice over 5G/NR settings are enabled. With these settings, as a 5G customer with a 5G device, you can make voice calls using VoNR, provided you are in 5G coverage.

Question: Is a software update required to enable VoNR/Vo5G?
Answer: Yes, many devices need a software update to enable VoNR. You can check for the latest updates in your phone’s settings.

Question: How do I enable/disable VoNR/Vo5G on my device?
Answer: VoNR can be enabled in your phone’s settings under 'Mobile Networks', often listed as 'VoNR/Vo5G/Voice over 5G SA'. If unavailable, your device may not support it yet. Just ensure that your mobile is updated with the latest software released by your device manufacturer. You can confirm the same by visiting the manufacturer's website or at their service centre.

Question: What happens if I move from a 5G to a 4G area during a VoNR call?
Answer: Your call will seamlessly switch from VoNR to VoLTE if you move out of 5G coverage to a 4G coverage area, ensuring no disruption.

Question: What is CNAP (Calling Name Presentation)
Answer: The CNAP (Calling Name Presentation) feature is a new system in India that will display a caller's registered name on a recipient's phone, using official Know Your Customer (KYC) data from telecom operators. This initiative, spearheaded by the Department of Telecommunications (DoT) and the Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI), aims to combat spam, fraud, and cybercrime by providing a government-backed, verified caller ID service.

Question: Why is only Jio displaying Customer name and no other operators?
Answer: As per the guidelines issued by the Government of India, the Caller Name Display feature has been made mandatory for all telecom operators. This service is presently in the trial phase and is being rolled out in a phased manner across various regions. All operators are currently testing this feature in the same or different states as part of the implementation process.

Question: I provided my Aadhaar card details as proof and did not give consent for this information to be shared with anyone. No consent was obtained prior to displaying my name.
Answer:
As per the guidelines issued by the Government of India, the Caller Name Display feature has been made mandatory for all telecom operators. This service is presently in the trial phase and is being rolled out in a phased manner across various regions. 
 Please note that your privacy is protected. Only your registered name is shown, and no Aadhaar details are shared when you make a call.

Question: Why am I getting Caller name display on some of the calls I received not for all?
Answer: This service is currently in a trial phase across the mobile telecom operators, which is why you may see caller names for some calls but not all at this stage. Name display also has device dependency, kindly keep your phone software updated to use Calling Name Presentation services.

Question: When I am receiving call from someone, name is getting displayed (CNAP) in the incoming call. Request to stop display of my name, as I don’t want to show my name to new callers.
Answer: The name displayed is strictly based on the official ownership records of your connection, in compliance with the latest guidelines issued by the Department of Telecommunications (DoT), Government of India. As per Government guidelines, there is no provision for the caller to opt out or stop his / her name being displayed to the calling party.

Question: When I am calling someone, the display is showing an incorrect name / different name. I want to get it corrected.  My name spelling getting displayed on the call is different, how can I correct my name spelling?
Answer: In accordance with the recent guidelines issued by the Department of Telecommunications (DOT), Government of India, the display of the caller's verified name is now mandatory for all connections. when you place a call to a recipient who does not have your number saved, the name displayed on their screen is automatically sourced from the official ID proof submitted at the time of your connection activation.  Currently, there is no provision to change or correct the displayed name. If any such name changes or correction process is notified by the government and made available in future, we will promptly update through our official media.

Question: I am using Jio Phone/Jio Bharat/ on My device, why is the caller’s name not showing on my screen?
Answer: This feature may work differently depending on the device. Currently, the Caller Name Display feature is not supported on JioPhone or JioBharat devices. Therefore, the caller’s name will not appear on these models.

Question: How CNAP (Caller name presentation) will show my mobile number if CLIR (Caller line identification restriction) functionality is active.
Answer: CLIR active connections will continue to display as “Private”.

Question: I am using iPhone unable to activate / deactivate call forwarding suddenly after CNAP (Caller Name Presentation) display facility enablement.
Answer: CNAP (Caller line name presentation) functionality is getting rolled out as per directions from DOT. This feature is now getting tested at Jio network to prevent suspected fraud, however displaying name also has device & firmware dependency. We recommend you to upgrade your firmware version, we hope that will solve the issue. Alternatively, you may connect with your device manufacturer to solve the issue.

Question: Why CNAP (Caller line name presentation) is not live in our state / location? By when CNAP will go live in our area.
Answer: CNAP (Caller line name presentation) functionality is getting rolled out as per directions from DOT, Haryana has been chosen as the first Licensed Service Area for the trial. It will get deployed progressively at your locations too. You will get notifications through official media channels as & when CNAP gets rolled out in your area too.

Question: Does CNAP (Caller name presentation) work when calling from WhatsApp or other VoIP apps?
Answer: No, CNAP applies only to regular mobile network calls.

Question: I don’t want caller name displayed when someone is calling me. How can I disable it / switch off this service?
Answer: Currently, there is no provision to opt out or stop the CNAP (Caller line name presentation) service by the called party. As and when, such CANP opt out process is notified by the government and made available in future, we will promptly update through our official media.

Question: If I’ve saved a contact in my phone, will your service still show the registered name or my saved name?  or When I receive a call from someone whose number I’ve saved, which name will appear — the one from my phonebook or the one registered in your Caller Name Display service?
Answer:
If you’ve already saved the number in your phonebook, your phone will always show the name you saved. 
 When the number is not saved in your contacts, the name you see is taken from the customer’s official ID used during SIM activation as per Govt guidelines.

Question: What is the Jio Customer Care Number for Jio and Non-Jio Users?
Answer:
Jio provides  24x7 customer care and call center support  through toll-free numbers for both Jio and non-Jio users. 
 
 Jio users  can call  199  from their Jio mobile number. 
 Non-Jio users  can call  1800-889-9999  from any network in India. 
 
You can contact  Jio customer care executives  for help with recharge and billing issues, data plans, SIM activation or replacement, network and connectivity problems, JioFiber services, and general support queries. Calling these numbers is  free of cost , and no balance is required.

Question: How do I contact Jio complaint helpline for network or service issues?
Answer: Call the Jio Complaint Number – 198.  You can dial  198 (available 24x7)  to report  network issues, call drops, billing errors, or mobile service complaints .  This is the  official Jio helpline number  to register  grievances or connectivity-related problems  directly with the  customer care  team.

Question: What is the Jio recharge enquiry number for prepaid users?
Answer: The Jio Recharge Enquiry Number is 1991.  Dial  1991 from your Jio mobile  to instantly check your  Jio recharge status, active plan details, data balance, and validity.  This number provides  automated updates  about your current  Jio prepaid pack and renewal reminders.

Question: How can I contact Jio for porting or new SIM requests?
Answer: Call 1860-893-3333 for Jio porting and new SIM support.  This number connects you directly to the  Jio sales and support team  for  Mobile Number Portability (MNP)  or  new SIM inquiries.  You can also ask about  Jio prepaid, postpaid, or Fiber plans.

Question: How can I activate my new Jio SIM card?
Answer: Dial 1977 to activate your new Jio SIM.  Use  1977  for  SIM activation, tele-verification, and enabling HD voice and data services.  This number helps you  complete KYC verification  and start using your  new Jio connection  immediately.

Question: How is JioTele OS different from other operating systems for TV?
Answer: JioTele OS  is built in India, crafted for India. It offers AI-powered content and regional recommendations from 10+ OTT apps, the largest collection of TV channels, multilingual voice support, and a single remote that works across all platforms.

Question: Do I need fast internet to use JioTele OS?

Question: What are the additional requirements for Wi-Fi Calling on iPhone?

Question: Can I use Wi-Fi Calling in International Roaming?

Question: Can I make a Wi-Fi Calling during domestic roaming?
Answer: Yes, you can make Wi-Fi calls while roaming anywhere in India.

Question: Who is eligible for Wi-Fi calling?
Answer:
In order to be eligible for  Wi-Fi Calling , you need to:
 
 Have a smartphone that is Wi-Fi Calling capable 
 Have an active Jio  tariff plan

Question: How much does Wi-Fi Calling cost?
Answer:
The  Wi-Fi Calling  feature is available at no additional charge and is included in your existing voice plan 
 Wi-Fi calls to international numbers are billed as ‘International Calls’ (also known as ISD calls).

Question: How do I make Wi-Fi calls?
Answer: Once you set up  Wi-Fi Calling , you have to follow the same process as regular voice calls. Your phone will automatically toggle between Wi-Fi and the  cellular network  to provide you with a seamless experience while calling.

Question: Do I need to keep VoLTE switched on to make a Wi-Fi call?
Answer:
Yes, it is recommended that you keep both VoLTE and  Wi-Fi Calling 
 switched on for a seamless experience.

Question: Will my recharge balance be deducted on making a Wi-Fi call?
Answer: Yes, a Wi-Fi call will be treated like a regular voice call.

Question: Does Wi-Fi Calling consume data? How much data is consumed during a Wi-Fi call?
Answer:
Yes,  W-Fi calling  consumes data from the WiFi network you are connected to 
 It does not consume your Jio 4G data 
 Voice calling uses less than half MB of data per minute 
 
 The actual data usage of your voice or video call may vary.

Question: How do I know if my handset is capable of Wi-Fi Calling?

Question: Why can't I make or receive Wi-Fi calls?
Answer:
If you are unable to make or receive Wi-Fi calls, it might be due to one of the following reasons:

 
 You have not upgraded to the latest device software update available. 
 The Wi-Fi Calling setting is turned off in your Device Settings. Visit our webpage to learn how to activate  Wi-Fi Calling . 
 You are not eligible to use Wi-Fi Calling. Please check the eligibility requirements. 
 You do not have access to a high-speed and stable Wi-Fi internet connection.

Question: Can I make Wi-Fi calls to emergency numbers or short codes that use location services?

Question: What is JioTunes?
Answer: JioTunes is a caller tune. You can select JioTunes from our large collection of songs ranging from Bollywood to International, Devotional to Regional and many other genres. Get MyJio app from Play Store/App Store & set the song of your choice as JioTune.

Question: How can I search for songs to set as my JioTune?

Other ways to search & set your JioTune: 
 
 
 SMS the first three words of the song of your choice to 56789. You will receive the list of songs carrying those three words and the instructions on how to set your selected song as your JioTune.

 You can also simply send JT to 56789 and follow the instruction to set a particular song as your JioTune.

Question: How do I activate JioTunes on my smartphone?
Answer:
You can activate JioTunes using one of these easy methods -
 
 Through  MyJio  app 
 Through  JioSaavn  app 
 IVR 
 Copy JioTune from another Jio customer 
 By SMS 
 
 MyJio app: 
 
 Download MyJio app from Play store or App store 
 Tap 'JioTunes' under 'Quick Links' 
 Search the song of your choice, listen to preview and select ‘Set JioTune’ 
 Get confirmation screen and SMS on activation 
 
 JioSaavn app:

 Tap 'JioTunes' under 'Trending Now' 
 Search the song of your choice, listen to preview and select ‘Set JioTune’ 
 Get confirmation screen and SMS on activation 
 
IVR:

 
 Dial 56789 from the Jio number on which you wish to set JioTune

Copy JioTune of another Jio customer:

Question: Is JioTunes service supported on all handsets?

Question: What is the period of validity of an active JioTune ?
Answer: A JioTune which has been set on a mobile number will remain active for 30 days. After 30 days, it will be deactivated automatically.

Question: When my call gets into waiting, can the caller hear my JioTunes?
Answer: In case your call is on wait, the caller will be listening to the call waiting default message.

Question: I am a paid user. What if I deactivate a JioTune before 30 days validity of a JioTune ?

Question: I am a free user. What if I deactivate a JioTune before 30 days validity of a JioTune ?
Answer: If a free user deactivates a JioTune before 30 days, then the user will have to wait for the balance number of days before setting a new JioTune. For example, if a free user deactivates the JioTune on the 27th day, he will have to wait 3 more days before he can set a new JioTune.

Question: I am a paid user. Will my JioTune get deactivated automatically?
Answer: For free and paid users both, an active JioTune will be deactivated automatically after 30 days.

Question: I am a paid JioSaavn customer. How many JioTunes can I set ?
Answer: Users who have JioTunes plus, JioSaavn Pro or Pro student subscriptions can set unlimited JioTunes.

Question: How to switch from a physical SIM to eSIM or transfer a Jio eSIM from one eSIM-ready device to another via the MyJio app?
Answer:
Whether switching from a physical SIM to eSIM or transferring your Jio eSIM from one eSIM-ready device to another, just follow the steps below on MyJio.    
 
 Step 1: Open the  MyJio  app, search for 'eSIM' in the search bar and then select the 'Switch to eSIM' option. 
 Step 2: Verify your email address and proceed. Then, enter the EID of the device where you want to activate the Jio eSIM. 
 Step 3: Authenticate your request by entering the OTP sent to your Jio number to initiate the eSIM request. 
 
 Once the eSIM request has been initiated, there will be a 2-hour cooling period to process your request. During this time, you can continue using the existing services on your Jio number. 
 After the cooling period is completed, you will receive an IVR call for confirmation of your eSIM request. Following the prompts, give your confirmation on the call and complete the request. 
 You'll receive an eSIM profile notification on your device or an eSIM profile QR code via email from notification@jio.com at your email ID. Simply follow the instructions to install the eSIM on your device.You can also refer to the  step-by-step process guide  for more details.

Question: What is JioEverywhereConnect service for cellular smartwatch/companion devices?
Answer:
It enables you to share the same Jio number on your smartphone and smartwatch.

Question: How do I pair my cellular smartwatch with my smartphone?
Answer:
For Apple Watch : 

Note: Before you proceed, make sure your phone is connected to a mobile network or Wi-Fi 

1. Launch the ‘Watch’ app on your iPhone 

2. Select ‘Mobile Data’ and tap ‘Set up Mobile Data’ 

3. Enter Jio credentials on the login page 

4. Tap on ‘Login’ to submit the details and continue to process 

5. Select the terms and conditions check box and tap ‘Continue’ 

6. Tap on ‘Done’ to acknowledge the receipt of confirmation 

7. Proceed further by tapping on ‘Continue’ 

8. Select ‘Mobile Data’ and here you will see ‘Jio’ under ‘Data Plan’ 
 
 For Samsung Watch : 

Note: Before you proceed, make sure your phone is connected to a mobile network or Wi-Fi 

1. Launch the ‘Galaxy Wearable’ app on your smartphone 

2. Select your device (Galaxy Watch) from the displayed options and finish the pairing process via Bluetooth 

3. On the ‘Home’ tab, tap ‘Mobile Networks’ and then tap ‘Next’ 

4. Enter Jio credentials on the login page 

5. Tap on ‘Login’ to submit the details and continue to process 

6. Tap 'Connect' on the next screen to complete the process.

Question: What are the benefits of Jio's 4G internet service?
Answer:
With Jio’s  High Speed Internet service  you can experience consistent high speeds, which means no more waiting for apps or web pages to get downloaded. 

You can upload and download your favourite pics and videos almost instantly, and even watch your favourite movie in High Definition without any buffering.

Question: What is 4G LTE service provided by Jio?
Answer:
Jio’s 4G LTE service is a high-speed mobile internet service that runs on fourth-generation 4G mobile technology. If you have ever wondered what 4G is, it simply refers to the next level of mobile networks after 3G that is designed to give you faster downloads, smoother streaming, and clearer voice calls. 
 Jio’s 4G service uses 4G LTE (Long Term Evolution) technology, which is an advanced form of 4G network performance. LTE makes your internet even faster by improving LTE speed, reducing delays, and giving you a stable connection even when you move between locations. This is why many people also ask what 4G LTE is. It’s basically the upgraded version of regular 4G that delivers better 4G network speed for both data and voice. 
 With Jio’s 4G LTE network, you get: 
 - Strong and consistent 4G speed for browsing, apps, social media, and gaming. 
 - Clear, high-definition voice calls powered by VoLTE. 
 - Fast access to videos, OTT platforms, and cloud services. 
 - Reliable 4G net performance across India. 
 Wondering what 4G technology is or what a 4G network is? In simple words, it is a modern mobile network standard that offers quick, smooth, and high-quality internet. Jio builds on this with its powerful LTE service, giving you one of the fastest and most dependable 4G LTE experiences in the country.

Question: Is signal strength critical for good internet speeds ?
Answer: A stronger signal indicates better signal strength and not necessarily signal quality. Speed is a function of various parameters, like the website being accessed, number of concurrent users, signal quality etc. You may get  good internet speeds  even on low signal strength.

Question: What should I do if my Jio mobile internet speed is slow?
Answer:
If you are experiencing slow mobile internet, there could be several reasons. 
    MyJio App offers a Troubleshoot feature that checks your plan validity, data balance, mobile settings and network status to help you diagnose the problem. 
 Steps to Troubleshoot Using MyJio App 
 
 Open MyJio App on the same number where you are facing the issue 
 Navigate to Jio Care → Troubleshooting 
 Select Slow Internet Speed and follow the on-screen instructions 
 
 If you don't have the MyJio App, click  here   to download. 
 Possible Reasons for Slow Internet Speed & How to Check 
 
 1. Plan Validity 
 
 Ensure you have an active plan on your Jio number. 
 Use MyJio App to check your plan details and high-speed data balance. 
 
 2. Data Balance Exhausted 
 
 If your high-speed data is exhausted, speed will drop to 64 Kbps. 
 To restore high-speed internet, recharge with a suitable data pack. 
 
 3. Signal Availability 
 
 Check if your device is receiving Jio signal. 
 Ensure the Jio SIM is inserted properly and Mobile Data is active on the Jio SIM. 
 Restart your device—this often clears temporary glitches in the device and reconnects to the network. 
 
 4. Device Issue 
 
 Try inserting your Jio SIM in another phone. 
 If internet works fine there, the issue may be with your device settings. 
 
 5. Network Congestion 
 
 Internet speed may reduce during peak hours when many users are connected to the same tower. This is common during evenings, weekends, or crowded events. Such slowdowns are temporary and usually improve over time. 
 
 6. Weak Indoor Coverage 
 
 Even if outdoor coverage is strong, indoor signals may weaken due to thick walls, metal structures, or reflective glass. 
 Moving closer to windows or open areas may improve speed. 
 
 7. Background App Usage 
 
 Apps may update, sync, or download content in the background. 
 This can consume bandwidth and slow down browsing or streaming. 
 
 8. Outdated Device Software 
 
 Old OS versions or firmware may cause network performance issues. 
 Updating your device can improve stability and speed. 
 
 9. Multiple Devices Using the Same Connection 
 
 If several devices are connected via hotspot or tethering, speed may reduce. 
 Heavy usage on one device can impact others 
 
 Watch our video guide for step-by-step troubleshooting video  here 
 Need further assistance, reach out to us anytime — we're always here for you 
 Reach out to us anytime through any of the Jio support channels below — we are always here for you: 
 
 Download  MyJio App  and access Jio Care 
 Call Us:  199  /  198 
 WhatsApp us:  7000770007

Question: What should I do if I am unable to connect to Jio True 5G?
Answer:
If you are using a JioTrue5G compatible handset and in an excellent 5G coverage area, you will automatically connect to 5G network. 
    If you are unable to connect to 5G, MyJio App offers a Troubleshoot feature to help you diagnose the issue. Open MyJio App, go to JioCare – Troubleshoot, and select ‘Unable to connect to 5G’. If you don’t have the MyJio App, download it here: http://tiny.jio.com/mobile 
 
 Here are the possible reasons you may not be able to connect to 5G, along with steps to help you fix the issue:
 
 Mobile Handset Compatibility 
 
	To enjoy Jio True 5G services, your Jio SIM must be inserted in a  5G-compatible handset What to do: 
 Check whether your handset supports 5G 
 Tap  https://www.jio.com/5g/ , scroll down and tap  “Check Now”  in the  Device Compatibility Check  banner. Then select your handset brand and model to verify 5G compatibility.
 
 
 
 Active Plan Validity 
 
	Ensure your Jio number has an  active recharge plan. What to do: 
 Check plan validity using the MyJio App 
 Verify your high-speed data balance to enjoy 5G speed 
 
 
 Mobile Network Settings (5G Not Enabled) 
 
	Ensure 5G is enabled in your mobile settings. 
 Android: 
 Settings → Mobile Network → Preferred Network Type → Select option with  5G / LTE / Auto 
 iOS: 
 For Dual SIM iPhones 
 Settings → Mobile Service → Tap Jio SIM → Voice & Data → Select  5G Auto 
 For Single SIM iPhones 
 Settings → Mobile Service → Mobile Data Options → Voice & Data → Select  5G Auto 
 
	Watch these helpful videos: 
	 For Android:   https://youtu.be/7gLcTs8QZ8I 
 For iOS:   https://youtu.be/KmtQNXK8r1U 
 
 5G Coverage at Your Location 
 Based on the available network (4G or 5G) signal strength of at your location, your handset will connect automatically to the best network to assure optimal experience.
	 
 Wi-Fi Turned ON 
 
	If your phone is connected to Wi-Fi, mobile data (5G) may not be used. What to do: 
 Turn OFF Wi-Fi and use mobile data 
 
 
 Power Saving / Low Power Mode Enabled 
	Power-saving modes disable 5G connectivity. Turn off power saving mode by following below steps: 
 Android: 
 Settings → Battery → Turn OFF Power Saving
	 iOS: 
 Settings → Battery → Turn OFF Low Power Mode
	 
 Software Update Pending 
 
	5G may require the latest handset software approved by the manufacturer. What to do: 
 Go to Settings → Software Update 
 Install any pending updates and restart the device 
 
 
 Network Mode Locked to 4G 
 
	If the device is locked to LTE/4G only, it will not connect to 5G. What to do: 
 Set network mode to  5G / LTE / Auto 
 
 
 Dual SIM Limitations 
 
	In some dual SIM phones, 5G works only on the  primary SIM slot What to do: 
 
 Set Jio SIM as:
				 Primary SIM for mobile data 
 Preferred SIM for network access 
 
 
 
 
 Temporary Network Issue / Outage 
 
	Temporary technical issues may affect 5G availability. You will automatically connect to 5G again once issue is fixed. What to do: 
 Restart the phone and try after some time 
 
 
 Refresh Network Connection 
 
	Refreshing the network can help re-register 5G. What to do: 
 Turn ON Airplane mode for 10 seconds, then turn it OFF 
 Restart the device

Question: What is the difference between simple voice and HD Voice?
Answer: HD Voice uses wide-band audio technology and noise cancellation. Due to this, voice conversations sound more natural with less background noise compared to standard voice calls. To experience HD voice, both the calling and receiving party should have handsets that support HD Voice.

Question: How can I deactivate call forwarding?
Answer:
To deactivate call forwarding on your mobile, you can either go to Settings > Call > Advanced settings > Call forwarding on your handset (please note that the navigation might differ), or dial the following short codes: 
 
 Call forwarding Unconditional - *402 
 Call forwarding - no answer- *404 
 Call forwarding - busy - *406 
 Call Conditional call forwarding - not reachable- *410 
 All forwarding - *413 
 
 You can also deactivate the call forwarding service through the MyJio app by following the steps below: 
 
 Open the  MyJio  App and log in with your number 
 Go to Profile > Mobile Settings > Service Settings 
 Select Call Forwarding and tap on Voice or Video, whichever is activated 
 Choose the active call forwarding type and complete SIM verification 
 Tap Disable, then select “Yes, disable” to confirm

Question: How can I activate call forwarding service?
Answer:
You can activate call forwarding directly from your handset by going to Settings > Call > Advance settings > Call forwarding. Please note that the navigation might differ on different handsets. 
 You can also activate Call forwarding service from MyJio app by following the steps below: 
 
 Open  MyJio  and log in using your number. 
 Go to Profile > Mobile Settings > Service Settings 
 Select Call Forwarding and tap on Voice or Video, depending on which type of call forwarding you want to activate 
 Choose the call forwarding type and complete SIM verification 
 Enter the 10-digit number to which you want to forward your calls 
 Tap on “Continue” to activate call forwarding 
 
 Your call forwarding service will be activated immediately. 
 Note: 
 
 Call forward service is free of cost. However, you need to have an active plan to activate the Call Forward/Divert services. 
 Call forward/divert can be done to all mobile and landline numbers (except BSNL numbers, own Jio number, and international numbers).

Question: What are the different types of call forwarding?
Answer:
There are 4 types of call forwarding offered by Jio, which you can enable directly from your handset: 
 • Call Forwarding Unconditional: All calls to your Jio number will be forwarded to the 10-digit number configured by you. 
 • Call Forwarding No Answer: In case of no answer from your Jio number, calls will be forwarded to the 10-digit number configured by you. 
 • Call Forwarding Busy: In case your Jio number is busy, calls will be forwarded to the 10-digit number configured by you. 
 • Call Forwarding Not Reachable: In case your Jio number is not reachable, calls will be forwarded to the 10-digit number configured by you. 
 Note: iPhone settings only support unconditional call forwarding.

Question: What is the indication for Jio HD voice or VoLTE on smartphones ?
Answer: Depending on the 4G smartphone, when it is connected to the Jio network, you will see one of these indicators - a VoLTE tag, an HD tag or horizontal arrows along with signal strength indicator on status bar. This indicates that your smartphone and  SIM  is connected to Jio network and you can make/receive voice/video calls.

Question: Do my Voice calls consume data from the data plan?
Answer: Your voice calls don't consume data from your 4G data plan. Voice calls are completely free.

Question: Do my Video calls consume data from the data plan?
Answer: Video calls consume data. Depending on the duration of the video call, the data is consumed from the 4G data plan. Video call consumes minimal data. A two minute video call on an average consumes 11.5MB of data from your plan data balance.

Question: Do I need to make changes in the device settings for enabling VoLTE?
Answer: Yes, you need to turn on VoLTE. Go to Settings > Mobile Data > Mobile Data Options > Enable LTE. If Voice & Data is off, tap it to turn on VoLTE.

Question: Will I be able to use Voice services if I switch off data on my Jio SIM?
Answer: You will be able to make or receive voice calls, video calls and also send or receive SMS even if data is switched off on your Jio SIM being used in a VOLTE phone.

Question: Can I make a call and browse the web at the same time with my VoLTE device?
Answer: You can make a voice or a video call on the Jio network and simultaneously browse the web.

Question: What are call management services?
Answer: Call management services are supplementary set of services that help you manage voice and video calls in an efficient way.  Jio  supports a wide range of call management services, such as Call Forward, Call Wait, Caller line identification (CLIP) etc.

Question: Which are the different call management services offered by Jio?
Answer:
Jio  offers the following call management services:
 
 CLIP (Caller Line Identification Presentation) - Displays the number of the person calling you. This is a pre-activated feature for all Jio customers 
 Call Wait - Informs your callers to wait, you can dial *411 Activate and *412 to deactivate 
 Call Hold - Pre-activated feature from network and allows you to put a call on hold and switch to make or receive another call. It is available for both voice and video calls 
 Call Forwarding - Allows you to forward your calls to another number

Question: What is multi-party voice call conferencing service?
Answer: Multi-party conferencing service allows you to set up voice and video conference calls. You can make 5+1 party voice call conference. This is a pre-activated feature for all Jio customers.

Question: How can I setup a voice conference call using my Jio Smartphone?
Answer:
To set up a voice conference call, follow the below mentioned steps:

 
 Initiate a standard voice call to the first contact 
 While on that call, click on 'Add Call' to call the second party 

	Note: When the second call is being established, the first call will be automatically be put on hold. You can swap between the two calls whenever you want to. 
 Tap Merge to join the two calls and form a 3-party conference call 
 You can repeat the above step to add upto 5 participants to your conference call

Question: How do I end a voice conference?
Answer: Conference participants (except for the initiating party) can easily opt out of the conference call mid-way by simply disconnecting the call. However, to terminate the conference completely, the person initiating the conference has to disconnect the call.

Question: Can I call any number on voice conferencing?
Answer: Yes, you can call any mobile or landline number as part of a voice conference. Both local and STD calls can be included in a conference. Conference facility can also be used while roaming on Jio's  4G LTE network .

Question: What is the call hold and resume feature?
Answer: The call hold feature helps you receive or make a second call (while on a current call) by putting the first call on hold. It can also be used to swap between the two ongoing calls.

Question: How can I activate the call hold service?
Answer: The call hold service comes pre-activated on all  Jio SIMs .

Question: What are the charges/ amount of data consumed for making a video call?
Answer:
Currently, there are no charges which are applicable for making a video call. However, data used for all video calls will be part of the daily High Speed Data (HSD) limit/quota (as per the plan customer chooses).

Post the data allowance is over data will be throttled and video calls will be barred. If customer attempts to make a video call (when data is throttled), it will by default get converted into a voice call.

Question: What is a Video call?
Answer: Video calling service lets you make an HD Voice call combined with real-time video. Making a video call is as simple as making traditional voice call. If the person you are calling can accept a video call, you will see a camera icon. Just select the contact and click on camera icon to initiate a video call. If you are on a voice call, you can click on the camera icon to upgrade to video call. In order to make a video call, both you and the person you are calling must use a VoLTE compatible  4G  handset.

Question: What are the steps to start a video call?
Answer:
In order to make a video call, both you and the person you are calling must use a VoLTE compatible  4G  handset or should have installed the Jio Join app and must be connected to Jio  4G network . You can initiate a video call in any of the following ways:

 
 Contacts/Address Book: Select a contact and tap the camera icon 
 Phone Dialler: Enter a mobile phone number of a contact that is capable of video calling and tap the camera icon 
 Call History logs: Select a phone number indicated with a camera icon 
 During an HD voice call: You can upgrade an HD Voice call to a Video call by tapping the Switch icon on the in-call screen. Video call will be established only after the called party accepts the video call request. On some handsets, the upgrade option is available under 'Modify Call' setting. Click on Modify call and select Video call.

Question: Can I make a video call if my data connection is turned off on Jio sim?
Answer: You can make a video call or switch from voice to video call even if your data connection is turned off on Jio SIM being used in a VoLTE device.

Question: Can I make Video calls to non-Jio mobile or landline numbers?
Answer: Currently, Video calling is only supported between Jio numbers. You cannot make or receive video calls to or from a non-Jio number. If you will initiate a video call to any non-Jio mobile or landline number, it will automatically convert into a voice call.

Question: How can I switch to a video call while I am making a voice call?
Answer: You can switch back and forth between high quality voice and high resolution video call modes in an on-going call. The call can be upgraded to a video call only after taking the consent of the other party on the call. Moving to a voice call does not require the permission of the other party. You can upgrade an HD voice call to a video call by tapping the Switch icon on the In-call screen. Video call will be established only after the called party accepts the video call request. On some handsets, the upgrade option is available under 'Modify Call' setting. Click on Modify call and select Video call to upgrade.
