Question: I am not able to send SMS from my Jio number. What do I do?
Answer:
You can try below mentioned troubleshooting steps: 
 
 If you are a prepaid user, check if you have an active  Recharge  plan and have SMS balance left in it. 
 If you are a postpaid user, check that your current usage is within assigned credit limit. 
 If you did a  SIM  change, your Incoming and Outgoing SMS service will remain barred for 24 hours as per Regulatory guidelines. 
 If you are a postpaid user with 10ARP plan, then you need to have an active Pre on Post Recharge plan to avail the Voice/Data or SMS services. 
 Have you exhausted your daily or monthly quota of SMS? If yes, please recharge with a Top-up voucher to send further SMS. 
 Ensure that the SMS Message Centre Number or Service Centre Number in your handset is updated. The message centre ensures that your text message is sent to the recipient. If the message centre number in your phone is blank, the text message can't be sent. This is normally updated under SMS Settings in your handset.
	   
 For Android devices in general: 
 To launch the SMS application on your phone: 
 Tap on  Menu  and go to  Settings 
 Scroll down and select  'Message Centre'  (If you cannot find Message centre in this list then look for and tap  Text message  and it should hopefully be in this list) 
 As soon as you open that, you'll be able to see and edit the message centre number. 
 If blank, enter in the number  1234 and click on OK . 
 Restart your phone  and send ‘test’ text to check if its working 
   
 If your Android phone doesn't have the  'Message Centre'  option available, then you can find the same by: 
 Typing  *#*#4636#*#*  into your phone on the dial screen 
 Select  Phone information 
 Scroll down and select the  SMSC  setting 
 Press  Refresh  to see the current setting 
 If the number is blank then type 1234 and press  Update 
 Restart your phone  and send send ‘test’ text to check if its working text to make sure it’s working 
   
 
 If you are still facing the issue, then try inserting your SIM card in a different handset that is known to send and receive SMS properly. If you can send and receive SMS in another handset, this means there may be an issue with the handset you normally use. 
 If you are unable to send an SMS to a specific number, but are able to send to other numbers, then it indicates that your phone settings and Jio services are working fine and there might be an issue with that specific number/operator. You can ask the recipient to check with their network operator. 
 Please use Jio Chat application to send any multimedia message(s).

 While sending Text messages to international numbers, ensure to use the correct format. You should start with the country code, followed by the phone number. 
	Example: UAE country code is 971 (dialing pattern +971XXXXXXXX)

Question: I am unable to receive SMS on my Jio number from a specific number. What do I do?

If you are unable to receive SMS from a specific number, but are able to receive from other numbers, then it indicates that your phone settings and Jio services are working fine, and there may be an issue with that specific number/operator. You can ask the sender to check with his / her network operator. 
 If you are not receiving OTP message from a specific bank/app, but are receiving messages from all other numbers, then it indicates your phone settings and Jio services are working fine, and this may be due to some issue with that specific bank/app. You can check with the concerned institution on this further. 
 In case of SIM change, your incoming and outgoing SMS service will remain barred for 24 hours as per Regulatory guidelines.

Question: What should I do if I’m unable to send or receive SMS on my mobile?
Answer:
If you are having trouble in sending or receiving SMS in your Jio mobile number, there could be a few possible reasons. The MyJio App offers a Troubleshoot feature that checks your device compatibility, settings, plan validity, and network status to help you diagnose the problem. 
 
 
Steps to Troubleshoot Using MyJio App
	 Open  MyJio App  on the same number where you are facing the issue 
 Navigate to Jio Care → Troubleshooting 
 Select ‘Unable to send SMS’ and follow the on-screen instructions 
 
 
 If you don’t have the MyJio App, download it here:  http://tiny.jio.com/mobile 
 Alternatively, you may check the following to solve the issue:
 
 
 
 Plan Validity 
 
	Ensure that your Jio number has an active plan with all benefits. Open MyJio App to check your plan benefits, validity & balance details What to do: 
 Check your current plan and validity using the  MyJio App 
 If you are using a  Postpaid connection , check whether any bill payment is pending 
 If you are a postpaid user, check that your current usage is within assigned credit limit. 
 If you are a postpaid user with 10ARP plan, then you need to have an active Pre-on Post Recharge plan to avail the Voice/Data or SMS services. 
 Have you exhausted your daily or monthly quota of SMS? If yes, recharge with a Top-up plan to send SMS messages 
 
 
 SMS Centre number 
 
 Ensure that the SMS Message Centre Number or Service Centre Number in your handset is updated. The message Centre ensures that your text message is sent to the recipient. If the message Centre number in your phone is blank, the text message can't be sent. This is normally updated under SMS Settings in your handset. 
 If your Android phone doesn't have the 'Message Centre' option available, then you can find the same by:
			 Typing  *#*#4636#*#*  into your phone on the dial screen
			 Select  Phone information 
 Scroll down and select the  SMSC  setting
			 Press  Refresh  to see the current setting
			 If the number is blank, then type 1234 and press  Update 
 
 Restart your phone  and send ‘test’ text to check if its working 
 
 
 SIM Change 
		SMS services are restricted for 24 hours after SIM/eSIM change.
		 This includes physical SIM change, eSIM conversions, or moving an eSIM to a new device. Please wait 24 hours if you have recently made any SIM change.
	 
 Network Coverage issue (Weak / Poor signal) 
 
 Restart your phone 
 Turn  Airplane Mode ON  for a few seconds, then turn it  OFF 
 Insert your Jio SIM into another handset where SMS send and receive service working properly. If you can send and receive SMS in another handset, this means there may be an issue with the handset you normally use.

Question: How is my SMS usage calculated on my plan?
Answer:
SMS usage is calculated based on the number of messages sent from your phone. While your phone allows you to type and send long messages, each SMS is counted in segments of 160 characters. So a message longer than 160 characters will be counted as 2 or more SMS. 
 If you are sending a message in a language other than English, the character limit per segment reduces to 70 characters, as these languages use a different encoding standard. 
 Please note that some apps also send SMS automatically to verify your phone number or device, these are counted as part of your SMS usage as well. 
 To check your SMS allowance and remaining balance, visit the  Usage  section in the MyJio app or log in to your account on Jio.com.

Question: What is DND?
Answer:
DND (Do Not Disturb) is an initiative by TRAI (Telecom Regulatory Authority of India) to save telecom consumers from unsolicited calls/SMS & spam. If a Jio user registers their mobile number with DND service, then it would be illegal for telemarketers to send any sort of unsolicited SMS or calls on those numbers.
	 You can register your DND preferences in any of the following ways: 
 
 To set your DND preference, open  MyJio  app, tap on ‘More’ option below your mobile number and select ‘Do not disturb’ option OR 
 Login to your account by clicking  www.jio.com/myaccount , and select ‘Do Not Disturb’ option under Settings and Service Settings OR 
 Send SMS START 0 to 1909 OR 
 Dial 1909 
 
	Upon registering your DND preference, you will get a confirmation SMS with a unique registration number and your DND preference will be updated in 24 hours. Once a preference is set, it can be modified after 24 hours.

Question: How do I activate Do Not Disturb (DND)?

You can activate DND using MyJio app or Jio.com: 
 Using MyJio App: 
 
 Open  MyJio App 
 Tap on Profile Icon and select ‘Settings’ option 
 Tap on ‘Service settings’ 
 Tap on ‘Do not Disturb’ 
 Scroll down & choose the respective DND service which you want to Block and select ‘Save’ option 
 Tap on ‘Proceed’ to confirm 
 
 From Jio.com: 
 
 Visit  Jio Login page 
 Tap on ‘Settings’ icon on the home page 
 Tap on ‘Service settings’ 
 Tap on ‘Do not Disturb’ 
 Scroll down & choose the respective DND service which you want to Block and select ‘Save’ option 
 Tap on ‘Submit’ to confirm 
 
 Other options to Activate DND: 
 
 SMS: Send START 0 to 1909. 
 Call: Dial 1909 from your Jio number. 
 
 You will receive a confirmation SMS with a unique registration number on submitting the request for DND activation. Your DND preference will be updated within 24 hours.

To raise a DND complaint, open  MyJio  app, tap on ‘More’ option below your mobile number, select ‘Do not disturb’ option and tap on ‘Complaint’. Alternatively, you can login to your account on  www.jio.com  and access DND section under Settings and Service settings to raise a DND complaint.

Question: What is a public Wi-Fi hotspot?

If the problem still persists, follow these steps:

Reboot your iPhone to take effect.

Question: What is the process to relocate my JioAirFiber connection?
Answer:
To relocate your JioAirFiber connection, you will need to raise a request on MyJio app or Jio.com (after logging in) by following these steps.
 MyJio App 
 
 Log in to  MyJio  App and select ‘Home’ 
 Tap on 'Relocate Connection' under useful links or click here  https://tiny.jio.com/ReloChat  to raise the Relocation Request 
 
 Jio.com 
 
 Visit  Jio.com  and click on the ‘Log in’ option on the top right of the screen.  
 Select ‘JioFiber’ and enter your Registered Mobile number (Validate using the OTP) 
 Scroll below to the ‘Useful links’ section and select ‘Relocate Connection’ 
 
 Here are some important points to note while Relocating your JioAirFiber connection 
 1. You must return the existing devices, as the connection will be disconnected based on the suspension date you select when submitting the relocation request. 
 2. A new connection will be provided at your new location. 
 3. When registering for the new connection, use the same registered mobile number from your old connection in order to redeem residual value voucher (Net of GST) if old devices has been submitted successfully. 
 4. The residual value voucher will appear in the ‘Voucher’ section of MyJio app within 90 days of your new connection’s activation if not redeemed during installation. 
 5.The new connection may be a JioFiber or JioAirFiber connection, depending on feasibility.

Question: Why am I being charged ₹1200 for the relocation process of my JioAirFiber connection?
Answer: As part of the JioAirFiber relocation process, the ₹1,200 charge is the booking amount for setting up a new  JioAirFiber  connection at your new location. However, this amount will be adjusted against your final plan payment. Your old connection will be disconnected, and any unused validity (Net of GST) will also be credited against RMN (Registered Mobile Number) in the form of Voucher, which can be used for plan payment of new installation or bill payments.

Question: Will I get my booking amount refunded if the new connection isn't activated when relocating my JioAirFiber connection?
Answer: Yes, if there is a technical issue from our end in setting up your new  JioHome  connection, then we will refund the booking amount to you. Please note that, as per JioAirFiber relocation process policy, the booking amount is refundable only if we are unable to service your new location..

Question: Is it mandatory to submit the device of my existing JioAirFiber connection for relocation?
Answer: Yes, you must submit the devices of your current  JioAirFiber  connection when relocating to a new address. As per the JioAirFiber relocation process, the residual value voucher for your remaining balance can be used only after the devices from your old connection are submitted

Question: What happens to my current plan if I relocate my JioAirFiber connection in the middle of the billing cycle?
Answer:
If you relocate your  JioAirFiber  connection in the middle of the billing cycle, a voucher will be issued based on the remaining days of your current plan after disconnection. For example, if your JioAirFiber plan costs ₹599 and you have 15 days remaining, you’ll receive a residual value voucher worth ₹300.   
 To be eligible for the redemption of residual value voucher: 
 
 The registered mobile number for both the old and new connections must be the same. 
 The new connection must be activated within 30 days of cancelling the old one. 
 Devices from the old connection must be submitted.

Question: Why can't I move the devices of my existing JioAirFiber connection to my new address?
Answer: Relocating an existing JioAirFiber connection to a new address is not feasible. Instead, a new  JioFiber  /  JioAirFiber  connection will be provided at your new location. If you're relocating, you can easily place a relocation request through the MyJio App. We’ll arrange a new JioAirFiber or JioFiber connection (based on the feasibility) at your new address to keep you connected without hassle. Your current connection will be terminated, and the remaining validity of your existing connection will be credited to your account against RMN (Registered Mobile Number) in the form of Voucher which can be redeemed against a positive bill amount or plan payment of new connection if old devices has been submitted successfully.

Question: Do I need to submit my documents again for relocating my JioAirFiber service?
Answer: Yes, you need to submit a new set of documents for KYC at the time of  JioAirFiber  installation at your new address. The KYC documents for your new JioAirFiber connection include your proof of identity and proof of address.

Question: What is the JioFiber Postpaid Offer?

The  JioFiber Postpaid  Offer is an advance-rent plan that lets you enjoy JioFiber without paying anything upfront. It is designed to give you a worry-free broadband experience with uninterrupted service and simple monthly billing. 
 Here’s what the offer includes: 
 
 
 Zero upfront entry cost
 
 You can get  JioFiber  installed without paying any security deposit or installation charges. 
 The usual ₹1,500 security deposit is waived. 
 Installation charges are waived on 3-month, 6-month, and 12-month postpaid plans. 
This means you start using fast fiber broadband with no initial cost.
 
 
 Always-on service
 
 With postpaid, your connection never stops. 
 Even if you miss your bill date, your internet continues. 
 No need to worry about recharges or expiry dates. 
This ensures you and your family stay connected at all times.
 
 
 Auto-pay for peace of mind
 
 You can enable auto-pay to make your monthly bill payments automatic. 
 No reminders. 
 No missed payments. 
 No interruption to service. 
Just set it once and enjoy complete convenience.

Question: Why do I have to pay a security deposit for JioFiber services?
Answer:
The security deposit for  JioFiber  is a refundable amount collected for the Jio Home Gateway (router) and any other Jio-provided devices installed at your home. This deposit ensures safe usage of the equipment and enables Jio to offer high-quality devices without charging you an upfront cost. 
 You will receive the security deposit back when you discontinue your JioFiber service, as long as all devices are returned in good and working condition. 
 
 
Important things to know: 
 The security deposit is fully refundable after device return and account closure. 
 All refunds for JioFiber are processed through NEFT directly to your bank account. 
 The refund process may take up to 60 days from the date you raise the cancellation request. 
 Make sure you return all devices, including the Jio Home Gateway, Set Top Box (if applicable), power adapters and cables, to avoid delays or deductions. 
 You can track your disconnection status and refund progress through the MyJio app or by contacting JioFiber Care. 
 
 
 The security deposit helps Jio maintain service quality and ensures you get reliable, high-performance broadband equipment at no additional upfront cost.

 Always on service:

 Highest quality service with 99.9% uptime

Question: Can I change my JioFiber Postpaid plan to JioFiber Prepaid?
Answer:
No, you cannot migrate from a JioFiber Postpaid plan to a  JioFiber Prepaid plan , right now. We will notify you in the  MyJio  app or by SMS as soon as this migration option becomes available. 
 
 Why this matters 
 You cannot switch directly because Postpaid and Prepaid billing, activation, and plan-management systems are different. This helps avoid billing or service interruptions while we add the migration feature. 
 
 
What you can do now
 If you want to move to a Prepaid plan immediately, you will need to close your Postpaid account and then activate a new JioFiber Prepaid connection. 
 Contact JioCare for help with the closure and activation process. 
 Keep your contact details updated in the MyJio app so you receive the migration update as soon as it’s launched. 
 Check current JioFiber plans and offers in the MyJio app before switching to find the best Prepaid option for your needs. 
 
 
 Need help? Open MyJio > Support > JioCare, or call JioCare for personalised assistance with plan changes and account closure.

Question: What is the advance rental that I will have to pay?
Answer:
JioFiber Postpaid  is offered only through long-term advance rental plans, so you will need to pay the rental amount upfront for 3 months, 6 months, or 12 months, depending on the plan you choose. This advance rental covers your full billing cycle for the selected duration. There are no monthly bills and no recurring payments during that period. 
 
 
When you pay the advance rental, you also get the added benefits of JioFiber Postpaid, such as:
 Zero security deposit for the router (ONT) and  Jio Set Top Box 
 Free installation on eligible plans 
 Access to OTT bundles, premium apps, and entertainment content (based on plan) 
 A seamless, uninterrupted service experience with no recharge reminders 
 Priority customer support for postpaid users 
 
 
 Your plan validity starts immediately after activation, and all benefits - internet, OTT, voice calling, and Digital TV (where applicable) - remain active for the entire advance rental period. 
 If you upgrade or migrate from prepaid to postpaid, your unused prepaid validity may also be adjusted through vouchers (as per eligibility).

Question: How can I make the payment for my postpaid JioFiber connection?

How to pay using the  MyJio  app:

How to pay on  Jio.com :

Question: Is e-bill mandatory if I opt for JioFiber Postpaid?

Question: Can I get hard copy of my JioFiber Postpaid bill?

Question: What is my JioFiber Postpaid bill cycle and can I change it?
Answer:
Your JioFiber  Postpaid  bill cycle is fixed based on the day your JioFiber connection gets activated. Jio assigns one of six bill cycles — 1st, 6th, 11th, 16th, 21st, or 26th of every month — and you can’t change this bill cycle after your connection is active. 
 
 You can easily check your JioFiber bill cycle anytime in the  MyJio  app or on  Jio.com  under the Billing section. 
 The bill cycle helps you know:
 
 When your monthly bill will be generated 
 When payment is due 
 How your usage is counted within each cycle 
 
 
 
 If you want more control over your bill payments, you can also:
 
 Set up auto-pay using UPI, credit card, or debit card 
 View past bills and download statements 
 Track real-time usage in the MyJio app 
 
 
 
 For plan changes, upgrades, or payment support, visit  JioFiber plans  or  Help & Support  on Jio.com.

Question: I opted for the newly launched JioFiber Postpaid Plan in the mid of the bill cycle. How much data will I get?

Question: When is the JioFiber bill generated for my JioFiber service and when is the due date for payment?

Question: How much late fee will be charged if I am unable to pay my JioFiber Postpaid bill by the due date?
Answer:
If you miss your JioFiber  Postpaid  bill due date, a late fee of ₹100 or 2% of the total outstanding amount, whichever is higher, will be applied.  
 This late fee will appear in your next month’s JioFiber Postpaid bill. This charge helps keep your account active and ensures uninterrupted service.  
 Your JioFiber connection will normally stay on, but repeated delays may impact your credit limit or future billing preferences. 
 
 
To avoid late fees, you can:
 Enable autopay through the  MyJio  app for worry-free monthly payments 
 Check your bill and due date anytime under the ‘Bills & Payments’ section 
 Update your registered mobile number to ensure you receive timely reminders

Question: How can I make the payment for my JioFiber Postpaid connection if it is barred or suspended due to Non-Payment of Dues?
Answer:
You can pay your JioFiber  Postpaid  bill even if your connection is barred or suspended due to non-payment of dues. Jio keeps all payment options active so you can clear dues quickly and restore your services. 
 You can make your JioFiber payment through the  MyJio  app,  Jio.com , UPI (Google Pay, PhonePe, Paytm, etc.), credit/debit card and net banking.  
 Once you pay the outstanding amount, your JioFiber Postpaid connection is usually restored within a few minutes. 
 
 Here are the steps to pay on the MyJio app:
 
 Open MyJio and sign in with your JioFiber number. 
 Tap 'Home' on the dashboard and select 'Pay Now'. 
 Choose your payment method and complete the payment. 
 
 
 
 Steps to pay on Jio.com:
 
 Go to Jio.com and sign in. 
 Open the JioFiber section and click 'Pay Bill'. 
 Select a payment mode and finish the transaction. 
 
 
 You can also enable AutoPay later to avoid future interruptions. If you need help, you can connect with JioCare by dialling 1800-896-9999 from your Registered Mobile Number.

Question: What is the applicable security deposit in the JioFiber Postpaid plans?
Answer:
With  JioFiber Postpaid , you get the convenience of long-term plans without paying any additional security deposit. All 3-month, 6-month, and 12-month advance payment JioFiber Postpaid plans come with zero security deposit for the ONT (internet box), the  JioFiber Set Top Box , and installation. 
 This means you only pay for the plan you choose. There are no extra upfront charges, no hidden fees, and no equipment deposits. Your devices are provided by Jio as part of your postpaid experience and remain active as long as your plan stays active. 
 
 
Choosing a long-term advance payment plan not only removes security deposits but also gives you:
 Faster installation 
 Seamless setup without paperwork 
 Premium OTT benefits on eligible JioFiber bundled plans 
 Consistent monthly billing with no recharge reminders 
 
 
 If you’re migrating from prepaid to postpaid, your existing equipment continues to work without any additional deposit.

Question: What is JioFiber?
Answer:
JioFiber is Jio’s high-speed fibre  broadband  service that delivers a superior internet experience for everything you do online — whether it’s streaming movies, gaming, working from home, video calling, smart home usage, or connecting multiple devices at once. It is designed to give you fast, consistent and reliable connectivity every day. 
 Unlike traditional broadband where the fibre cable reaches only your building, JioFiber brings fibre directly to your home. This 'last-mile fibre' approach ensures ultra-fast upload and download speeds, lower latency, better stability, and stronger performance even during peak hours. 
 
 
With JioFiber, you get:
 Ultra-fast internet speeds for smooth streaming, learning, video conferencing, and gaming 
 A stable and secure connection for all your smart devices 
 Bundled entertainment benefits on eligible plans with OTT apps, Digital TV channels, and more 
 JioFiberVoice for high-quality voice and video calling 
 Future-ready technology built to support next-generation digital experiences 
 
 
 Think of it as having a private high-speed internet highway running straight into your home, giving you a seamless digital experience across every screen and device.

Question: What is Fiber to the Home (FTTH) technology in JioFiber?
Answer: JioFiber connectivity comes directly to your home unlike in most cases where the cable reaches only the building and the last few metres of end connectivity are connected using a traditional cable which reduces the speed and user experience due to patches and inferior cable qualities of such patch up. With JioFiber reaching directly to your home, it is a completely different  broadband internet   experience.

Question: How is JioFiber technology better than my current broadband service?
Answer:
JioFiber  broadband service  is based on Fiber Optic technology. This technology offers a lot of advantages over the traditional cables:
 
 It provides faster internet connection. 
 Fiber networks are not required to be changed post-installation. The companies upgrade them with latest technology that creates the electronic light pulses without having the need to replace the fiber cables. 
 
 JioFiber’s  ultra-fast download and upload speeds enable seamless UHD Video services, video conferencing and a variety of camera applications.

Question: What is the Wi-Fi coverage provided by the Jio Home Gateway on the JioFiber network?
Answer:
The  JioHome  Gateway is designed to give strong, stable Wi-Fi coverage for most Indian homes. In typical conditions, it can comfortably cover an open area of around 1,000 sq. ft. on the same floor. This is ideal for apartments, 1–2 BHK homes, and compact office spaces. 
 
 However, actual Wi-Fi range can vary because wireless signals are affected by many real-world factors inside your home. 
 
 
What can affect your JioFiber Wi-Fi coverage?
 
 Several conditions may reduce or change the coverage area 
 Interference from nearby Wi-Fi routers in your building or neighbourhood 
 Router placement, especially if it’s inside a cabinet, corner, or behind objects 
 Number of walls between the Home Gateway and the device 
 Wall thickness and material (concrete walls weaken signal the most) 
 Distance from the router — signal drops as you move farther away 
 Too many devices connected at once 
 
These factors apply to all Wi-Fi networks, not just JioFiber.
 
 
 
How to improve JioFiber Wi-Fi coverage?
To get better indoor coverage, try the following:
 Place the Home Gateway in a central, open area 
 Keep it away from metal objects and inside-wall cabinets 
 Make sure no other routers are too close 
 Ensure your issue is not device-specific by checking signal bars 
 Use the MyJio → Run Diagnostics tool to check coverage in real time 
 
 
 
If you have a larger home or want seamless Wi-Fi in every room, you can also explore:
 Jio Extenders 
 Mesh Wi-Fi setup 
 JioAirFiber, which offers wider, whole-home coverage

Question: How many voice (landline) connections can I get with my JioFiber account?
Answer:
You get one  JioFiberVoice  landline number with each  JioFiber  account. This number is linked to your Jio Home Gateway and can be used for both voice and video calling. 
 
 Even though you receive one landline number, you can use it in multiple ways:
 
 You can connect one landline phone directly to your JioFiber router. 
 You can also set up the same JioFiberVoice number on up to six smartphones or Jio Set Top Boxes using the  JioJoin  app. 
 All devices will share the same landline number, giving you flexibility to make and receive calls from anywhere inside your home. 
 
 
 At any given time, one device will handle the active call, but all configured devices will ring for incoming calls. 
 This setup gives you the convenience of a traditional landline with the flexibility of smart calling across multiple devices, without needing extra wiring or separate landline connections.

Question: How can I apply for a new JioFiber connection?
Answer:
Getting a JioFiber broadband connection is quick and hassle-free. You can easily register online for a new  JioFiber  connection and enjoy ultra-fast internet, free voice calls, and access to OTT apps on your Jio Set Top Box. 
 Follow these simple steps to apply for JioFiber online: 
 
 Visit the official  JioFiber registration  page. 
 Enter your full name, mobile number and complete address where you’d like the JioFiber broadband connection to get installed, then click 'Generate OTP'. 
 Enter the OTP received on your registered mobile number and click 'Verify OTP'. 
 Click ‘Submit’ to complete your registration request. 
 
 
 
 Once your request is submitted, a Jio representative will contact you to confirm your details, explain JioFiber plans and installation options, and schedule a convenient time for a home visit and connection setup. 
 Additional Information: 
 
 Installation process: After address verification, the Jio team will install the JioFiber router (Jio Home Gateway) and, if applicable, the Jio Set Top Box (STB) for broadband and TV access. 
 Documents required: Keep your Aadhaar Card or any valid proof of identity and address (POI/POA) ready for KYC verification. 
 Available plans: You can choose from a wide range of JioFiber broadband plans, including options with unlimited data, OTT subscriptions, and 4K set-top box benefits. 
 Installation time: In most cities, JioFiber installation is done within 24 to 48 hours of registration, depending on local network availability. 
 
 
 Pro Tip: Download the MyJio app to track your installation status, manage your JioFiber account, and explore exclusive Jio offers once your connection is active. 
 Ready to experience blazing-fast fiber broadband? Register now for your JioFiber connection and enjoy high-speed internet, smart Wi-Fi, OTT apps, and crystal-clear 4K entertainment - all in one connection.

Question: What is the cricket season JioHome quarterly plan offer and what are its benefits?
Answer:
Under the cricket season JioHome quarterly plan offer, new JioHome users taking a new connection on or after 10th March 2026 with an eligible quarterly plan of Rs 2222 or Rs 3333 or Rs 4444 as the first recharge will be eligible for “1 extra month of JioHome service free with every 3-month recharge”.  So, customer will get 4 months service at the price of three months.  Customers enrolled under this offer can avail up to 3 free months extra i.e. 3 eligible quarterly plan recharges (including FRC).  
 To receive the bonus days on your FRC, 2nd and 3rd recharges, all three transactions must be completed within 9 months (exactly 270 days) from your original activation date.  
 In addition to the above benefits, customers enrolled under this offer, can also claim 18-month of Pro Google Gemini offer worth Rs 35,100, which includes 5TB Google cloud storage absolutely free.

Question: Who is eligible for the cricket season JioHome quarterly plan extra validity offer?
Answer: This offer is exclusively for customers who activate a new JioHome connection using the Rs 2222 or Rs 3333 or Rs 4444 as FRC (First Recharge) on or after 10th March 2026. Customers who activate on any other plans are not eligible for the bonus days, even if they later recharge with Rs 2222 or Rs 3333 or Rs 4444 plan. Existing users are not eligible for this offer.

Question: What is the start date of the cricket season JioHome quarterly plan offer?
Answer: This offer starts for new ORN generated on or after 10th March 2026. This is a limited period offer.

Question: How many total days of service do I get on my first recharge (FRC)?
Answer:
Eligible customers receive a total of 120 days on first recharge of eligible quarterly plan of Rs 2222 or Rs 3333 or Rs 4444.  
 
You get 90 days immediately upon activation. An additional 30 days will be placed in your "plan queue" and will automatically activate as soon as the first 90 days expire.

Question: What happens if I recharge with Rs 2222 or Rs 3333 or Rs 4444 for the 2nd or 3rd time?
Answer: You can recharge with any of the eligible plans (Rs 2222 or Rs 3333 or Rs 4444), 2nd and 3rd time to get 3 month + free 1 month benefit.

Question: Is there a time limit to claim these benefits?
Answer: Yes. To receive the bonus days for your FRC, 2nd and 3rd recharges, all three transactions must be completed within 9 months (exactly 270 days) from your original activation date. Bonus days will be credited within 7 days of activation/ subsequent eligible recharge.

Question: Do I need to manually activate the bonus "queued" days?
Answer: No. The system handles this automatically. Once your base plan (e.g., the initial 90 days) expires, the queued bonus (e.g., the extra 30 days) will start immediately without any action required on your part.

Note, users with a Jio number as JioHome registered mobile number (RMN) will be able to claim Pro Google Gemini benefits on MyJio app, 48 hours post activation of their JioHome connection. Users with a non-Jio RMN can claim the benefits on MyJio post 31st March 2026

Question: How do I redeem the discount voucher for my JioFiber multi-month advance payment?
Answer:
If you’ve made a multi-month advance payment for your  JioFiber Postpaid plan , you’re eligible to receive an exclusive discount voucher that can be redeemed directly from the MyJio app. These vouchers are part of Jio’s advance recharge benefits, rewarding users who make long-term plan payments upfront. 
 
 
Here’s how to redeem your Jio discount voucher:
 Open the  MyJio  app on your smartphone. 
 Log in with your JioFiber Registered Mobile Number (RMN). 
 Go to the ‘My Vouchers’ section under the main menu. 
 Select the voucher you wish to redeem. 
 Tap on ‘Redeem’ and confirm your selection. 
 
 
 Once redeemed, the voucher value will be automatically adjusted against your next bill or credited to your Jio account as per the offer terms. 
 
 
Important things to note:
 You can redeem the discount voucher only when your account shows a positive outstanding balance. 
 If you have made a 6-month advance payment, the voucher will become eligible for redemption after 6 months, once your advance balance is utilised. 
 Make sure your MyJio app is updated to the latest version to view all active vouchers. 
 The voucher details, such as value, validity period, and applicable services, are visible in the ‘My Vouchers’ section. 
 
 
 For example, if you recharge your JioFiber connection with a 6-month advance plan, your discount voucher will be available for redemption after 6 months, when your next billing cycle begins. 
 
Why redeeming vouchers matters:
 
Redeeming your Jio advance payment discount voucher ensures you don’t miss out on:
 Extra value or cashback on renewals. 
 Discounts on future JioFiber or Jio Postpaid recharges. 
 Seamless renewal without service interruption.

Question: What is JioFiber Plan Upgrade?
Answer:
JioFiber Plan Upgrade allows prepaid users to upgrade their plan by paying the difference amount of the New Plan and the Current Plan in the middle of the month. 
 In a nutshell: 

Amount Payable for Upgrade = New Plan Value - Unused Amount of Current Plan

Question: How can I upgrade my JioFiber Prepaid plan?
Answer:
You can simply upgrade your JioFiber Prepaid plan by following the below steps:

 
 Visit the Fiber section on the MyJio app 
 Tap on ‘View plan’ 
 Under the ‘My Plan’ section, click the ‘Upgrade Plan’ button 
 Select the new recharge plan you want 
 Your existing plan balance will get adjusted with your new plan’s payment

Question: I am a JioFiber Prepaid user. Am I eligible to upgrade my JioFiber plan?
Answer: Yes, JioFiber allows prepaid users to upgrade their plans. Just that the new plan you have selected should be of higher value than the unused amount of the current/queued plan. Also, the remaining validity of the current plan should be more than 1 day.

Question: I have taken a 12-month plan. Can I switch to a 3-month plan to avail of JioFiber Plan Upgrade?
Answer: Yes, you can switch to any plan of your choice, however the price of your selected plan should be higher then the remaining balance of your active plan. For example, if a customer is currently subscribed to the 399 3-month plan (₹1197), they will have the option to choose from all available plan variants for upgrade, starting from ₹1499 and above, including monthly plans.

Question: What amount will I have to pay if I upgrade my prepaid plan in the middle of my Current Plan?
Answer:
The unused amount and the remaining validity of your current plan will not be lost. You just need to pay the difference amount to upgrade. Let’s understand how the amount gets adjusted here below: 
 Current Plan: ₹699 for 1 month 

Total Plan Value with GST: ₹825 

Recharge Date: 2 October 

Plan Upgrade Date: 12 October 

Utilised Validity: 11 days 

Used Amount for 11 days: ₹302 

Unused Amount: ₹825 - ₹302 = ₹523 – (A) 
  

 New Plan : ₹999 for 1 month 
 New Plan Value with GST =  ₹1,179 – (B) 

 



 Amount Payable (A – B) : ₹1,179 – ₹ 523 =  ₹656 

 



 Note : Decimal values have been rounded off for simplicity of calculation.

Question: Can I upgrade my plans which are in the queue?
Answer: Yes, you have the flexibility to upgrade your plan which is in the queue. All you need to do is, select the plan which needs to be upgraded and the full amount of the queued plan will get adjusted with the new plan’s price (including GST).

Question: Can I upgrade my plan more than once while my current plan is still valid?
Answer: Yes, you can upgrade your plan at any point in time. There is no limit on the number of times you upgrade your plan.

Question: Can I migrate to the JioFiber Postpaid plan after doing a plan upgrade?
Answer: Yes, you can. The unused entitlements and OTT subscriptions of your current plan will stop at the time of migration. We will adjust the equivalent of the unused amount and validity of your current prepaid plan in your postpaid bill.

Question: Will there be any refund after I upgrade my plan?
Answer: No, we don’t provide refund. However, the residual balance of your current/queued plan will get adjusted with your upgraded plan.

Answer: No, you will be unable to opt for the JioFiber plan upgrade if you are on the Referral/Trial plan.

Question: Can I change my JioFiber plan or offer after activation?

 Open the MyJio app, go to 'Home', and tap on 'Plan details'. 
 Tap on 'Change plan'. You will see all the plans that are eligible for change. 
 Select the plan you want and complete the payment. 
 
 
 
When you request a JioFiber plan change:
 
 You will need to pay the advance rental and GST for the new plan and duration you select. 
 The amount you pay will be adjusted in your future bills, so nothing goes to waste. 
 Your new plan will start from 00:00 hrs the next day, ensuring a smooth transition without service interruption. 
 
 
 This allows you to instantly unlock higher speeds, more OTT apps, or enhanced Digital TV benefits if you choose an upgraded plan. 
 
Must-know: 
 
 You can change your JioFiber Postpaid bill plan only once per billing cycle, so choose your plan carefully based on your family’s internet and entertainment needs. 
 If you’re unsure which plan fits you best, you can explore speed tiers, OTT bundles, and long-term value options on Jio.com or inside the MyJio app before switching.

Question: What is the process to activate Amazon Prime Membership after choosing an eligible JioFiber plan?
Answer:
When you recharge with an eligible  JioFiber bundled plan , you can activate your Amazon Prime Membership in just a few simple steps. Once activated, Amazon Prime gives you access to Prime Video, ad-free music, faster deliveries, and exclusive member benefits at no extra cost. 
 Here’s how to activate your Amazon Prime Membership: 
 Check your SMS from Jio. 
 After your eligible JioFiber plan is activated, you’ll receive an SMS with a link prompting you to download or open the MyJio app. 
 
 
Open the  MyJio  app and tap 'Activate Now'
 Install or launch the MyJio app on your mobile.

Log in to your Amazon account

 Once you log in, your Amazon Prime Membership will be activated instantly under your JioFiber plan.

Question: I have been asked to pay advance plan charges at the time of the JioFiber bill plan change. What will happen to the advance payment made at the time of activation?
Answer:
Your advance payment and any subsequent payment made at the time of the plan change will get adjusted with the upcoming bills. You will be charged on prorated rent of the old plan till the bill plan change request date. From the date of the bill plan change, the new rent shall be applicable.. 
 Please refer to below example for a sample bill plan change request:

 
 The customer activates the JioFiber service on 1st January with ₹999 (12 months) advance rent plan. 
 Total payment made at the time of activation = ₹14,146 (999 * 12 + 18% GST) 
 The customer requested for bill plan change on 15th January to ₹1499 plan. 
 Total payment at the time of bill plan change = ₹1769 (₹1499 + GST) 
 Total payment by customer ₹14,146 + ₹1769 = ₹15,915 
 Total rent for 1st to 15th January = ₹570.4 (₹999 / 31days * 15 days) + GST 
 Balance available for adjustment in new ₹1499 plan = ₹15,344 (₹15,915 - ₹570.4)

Question: What is the Talkback Feature on Jio Set-Top Box and How to Use It?

If your Set-Top Box is reading out on-screen content and you didn’t enable it, don’t worry! You can turn the Talkback feature on or off by pressing and holding the ‘0’ key on your Jio remote for 5 seconds. This will immediately activate or deactivate voice prompts as needed.

Question: How do I connect the Jio Set Top Box over Wi-Fi?
Answer:
You can connect your  Jio Set Top Box  to your home Wi-Fi network in a few simple steps. This lets you stream apps, watch live TV, and enjoy entertainment even without an ethernet cable. 
 Just follow the steps below to connect your Jio Set Top Box over Wi-Fi:
 
 Unplug any ethernet cable if it’s currently connected to your Jio Set Top Box. 
 On your TV screen, go to Settings, or select the Wi-Fi icon on the home screen. 
 In the Settings menu, select Network. 
 Choose the Wi-Fi (SSID) you want your Set Top Box to connect to. 
 Enter the Wi-Fi password for that SSID. 
 
 
 After the connection is established, you’ll see a 'Successfully Connected' message on your TV screen. 
 Once connected, your Jio Set Top Box will automatically use this Wi-Fi network for streaming, updates, and smart TV features. Make sure your JioFiber Wi-Fi signal is strong in the room for the best viewing and streaming experience. 
 If you face connectivity issues, you can restart your router, move the Set Top Box closer to the Wi-Fi source, or manage your Wi-Fi settings through the MyJio app.

Question: How can I connect a Bluetooth device to my JioFiber's Set Top Box?

(If needed, refer to the device’s user manual.)

Question: How can I check the internet speed on my Jio Set Top Box?
Answer:
You can check the internet speed on your  Jio Set Top Box  using the Jio NetVelocity app, which is pre-installed on the device. NetVelocity gives you accurate details about your download speed, upload speed, network quality, and overall connection performance on your JioFiber network.

 Select Applications.

 Network strength and stability

Question: How do I set up my JioFiber Set Top Box remote?
Answer:
To set up your  Jio Set Top Box  remote, follow these steps: 
 
 Check the batteries:  Remove and reinsert the batteries (preferably new ones). 
 Factory reset the remote: 
 
 For 43-key remote: Long press the 7 + 9 keys for 4 seconds. 
 o For 38-key remote: Long press the OK + TV keys for 4 seconds. 
		(The remote's LED will blink twice to indicate a factory reset.) 
 
 
 Pair the remote with the Set Top box: 
 
 Reboot the STB by removing and reinserting the power adapter. 
 Place the remote close to the STB LED (within 30 cm). 
 Press any key once and wait for the LED to stop blinking. 
 After the blinking stops, press any key multiple times to complete the pairing process. 
		(Note: Once paired, the remote's LED will blink once along with the STB LED simultaneously when any key is pressed.)

Question: What to do if the Amazon Prime Video app is displaying HDMI error while playing any content or the HDMI resolution is falling?

If the Amazon Prime Video app is displaying an HDMI error or the resolution is falling, it might be due to dust settling in your HDMI port. Please follow the below steps to clean the HDMI port:

 
 Unplug the HDMI cable from the TV HDMI port 
 Now take a small brush and clean the TV HDMI port 
 Reconnect the HDMI cable to the TV HDMI port (Try with different TV HDMI ports) 
 Check if the issue has been resolved, if not, contact JioFiber Care and generate a service request by calling our toll-free number 1800-896-9999 or sending us a mail at JioFiberCare@jio.com

Question: What should I do if my TV screen connected to the Jio Set Top Box is showing a blurred image or the colours look mismatched?
Answer:
If your TV shows a blurred picture, washed-out colours, or unusual colour tones while using the  Jio Set Top Box , it usually happens because of a loose HDMI connection or incorrect display settings.  
 
 You can fix the picture clarity issues easily by following the steps below. 
 
 Check and clean the HDMI connection 
 Unplug the HDMI cable from your TV’s HDMI port. 
 Take a small brush or a clean cloth and gently clean the TV’s HDMI port to remove dust. 
 Reconnect the HDMI cable firmly into the same HDMI port. 
 If the issue continues, try plugging the HDMI cable into a different HDMI port on your TV. 
 Check whether the picture quality has improved.  
 
 
 
 If the picture still looks blurred or the colours remain off, try the second method below.
 
 Adjust the HDR settings 
 On the Jio Set Top Box, go to 'Settings'. 
 Select 'System Settings'. 
 Choose 'Display Settings'. 
 Choose 'HDR Settings' 
 You will see three options: Auto, On, Off. 
 Select Off to disable HDR. 
 Reboot the Set Top Box and check if the picture quality is now clear. 
 Disabling HDR often fixes colour mismatch issues caused by TV-model compatibility. 
 
 
 
 Still facing the issue?
If the problem continues after trying both methods, contact JioFiber Care to raise a service request.
 
 Call the toll-free number: 1800-896-9999 
 
A technician will help diagnose and fix the issue at your home.

Question: How can I check if there is any problem with my JioFiber broadband internet?

Here’s how to use Run Diagnostics:
 
 Download the  MyJio  app and link it to your  JioFiber  account. 
 Select 'Home' from the top menu.

Question: What is the process to migrate from JioFiber Prepaid to JioFiber Postpaid?

Question: How can I change the ownership of my JioFiber/JioAirFiber connection?

An ownership change request can be initiated for a blood relationship, spouse or legal heirs in case of voluntary transfer or an owner's demise. You can raise the request for ownership change through the MyJio app. Please follow these steps to submit an ownership change request:

 Click on 'Settings' and then 'Service settings' 
 Click on the 'Ownership transfer' icon 
 Select the appropriate reason 
 Verify your mobile number based on the OTP received 
 Select the relation from the drop-down menu 
 Enter valid details and capture the front and back of the document from your device’s camera or upload documents in a PDF format (Note: The file should be less than 2 MB) 
 Click and upload a photo of the new owner 
 Enter the OTP sent to the mobile number of the new owner 
 Click on Proceed to complete your request submission

Question: Why am I getting slow speed on my JioFiber connection?
Answer:
If your JioFiber internet is not working, you can check and troubleshoot it through  MyJio  App. Please follow below steps: 
 
 Log in to MyJio App. Select “JioFiber” 
 Scroll down to “JioCare: Help & Support” section 
 Click “Run Diagnostics” 
 Select “Internet Connectivity” (For issues with Wi-Fi Coverage click “Wi-Fi Coverage & Health) 
 Click on “Run Diagnostics” and follow instructions
	 Please note that Wi-Fi speeds are dynamic and can be affected by the following: 
 
 The number of wireless devices connected to the network at the same time. Connecting many devices to a network at the same time can reduce available bandwidth for each device. Reducing the number of devices connected to a  broadband network  might improve performance. 
 The distance between the device and access point. Too much distance from the Home Gateway can cause slower speeds over Wi-Fi 
 Speed depends on other factors like interference from other radios/microwaves between your device and Jio Home Gateway 
 Speed also depends on your device/handset specifications 
 
 
 
 Click for video to know How to improve Internet Experience over Wi-Fi

Question: How can I connect my Smart TV to the Jio Home Gateway on my JioFiber network?
Answer:
You can connect your Smart TV to your  JioFiber  network using either a LAN cable or Wi-Fi. Both options work well, and you can choose based on where your TV is placed and how strong your Wi-Fi signal is. 
 
 Option 1: Connect using a LAN cable (recommended for best stability)
 
 Plug one end of the Ethernet (LAN) cable into the LAN port of your Jio Home Gateway (router). 
 Plug the other end into your Smart TV’s LAN port. 
 Your TV should automatically detect the wired connection and connect to the internet. 
 
A wired LAN connection gives you faster, more stable internet - ideal for streaming movies, OTT apps, and HD/4K content.
 
 
 Option 2: Connect using Wi-Fi
 
 On your Smart TV, go to Settings → Network/Wi-Fi Settings. 
 Select your Wi-Fi Name (SSID) from the list of available networks. 
 Enter your Wi-Fi password to connect. 
 If your TV shows an option for Automatically Connect, enable it so your TV reconnects to JioFiber automatically every time. 
 
 
 For the best Wi-Fi experience, ensure your TV is placed within good range of the router, avoid physical obstructions, and keep other large appliances away from the Wi-Fi path. 
 Once connected, you’ll be able to stream OTT apps, watch live TV, play games, and enjoy smooth video playback through your JioFiber network. 
 If your TV is not connecting or the internet seems slow, you can use the Run Diagnostics feature in the MyJio app to check signal strength and troubleshoot issues.

Question: How can I connect my device to the Jio Home Gateway Wi-Fi network?
Answer:
To connect your device to the  Jio Home Gateway  Wi-Fi network, follow the steps below,depending on the type of device you have. 
   
 For Windows Laptop/PC 
 
 Turn Wi-Fi ON. If your computer has a physical Wi-Fi switch or function key, ensure it’s turned on. 
 This switch is usually on the side of the laptop or among the keyboard function keys. 
 If unsure, check your computer manufacturer’s support site. 
 Click the Wi-Fi icon on your taskbar. 
 Select your Jio Home Gateway network (SSID). 
 Check the 'Connect Automatically' box. 
 Click 'Connect'. 
 Enter your Wi-Fi password when prompted. 
 
 
 For MacBook

Question: What is JioFiberVoice?
Answer:
JioFiberVoice  is a digital voice and video calling service that comes with your JioFiber connection. It works through the JioFiber Home Gateway (router) and gives you a dedicated 10-digit JioFiberVoice number for calling across India.
 
 With JioFiberVoice, you can make crystal-clear voice calls, HD video calls, and enjoy a reliable landline experience without needing a separate telephone connection.  
 The service can be used on:
 
 One landline phone, connected to your Jio Home Gateway 
 Up to six smartphones through the JioJoin app 
 Jio Set Top Boxes, for TV-to-TV and TV-to-mobile video calling 
 
 
 Once activated, you get unlimited calling benefits and can use your JioFiberVoice number from multiple devices at home. Your mobile and TV devices work as extensions of your landline, giving you a full digital home calling experience. 
 JioFiberVoice activation is quick and can be done through the  MyJio  app during JioFiber setup or anytime later.

Question: What are the features of JioFiberVoice?
Answer:
Please take a note of the following features of  JioFiberVoice :
 
 One number, multiple devices:  You can now configure the same 10-digit JioFiberVoice number in a maximum of 6 smart devices. This includes: smartphones, Jio Set-top Boxes, and one landline phone. 
 In-house mobility for voice:  Use your smartphone to make/receive landline calls at your convenience. 
 Outside home mobility:  Set up ‘Parallel Ringing’ on a Jio mobile number to never miss a landline call. 
 Conferencing:  Audio and video conferencing services. 
 Supplementary services:  Enjoy other services such as call forwarding, call hold/resume, and audio-to-video upgrade/downgrade. These services would be available for JioFiberVoice users.

Question: What are the advantages of a JioFiberVoice connection over any other landline connection?
Answer: JioFiberVoice  offers HD voice and video calling over one landline device and up to six smartphones/Jio Set-top Boxes. It offers the flexibility of making calls at the place of your choice within your home.

Question: What are the advantages of a JioFiberVoice connection over a mobile connection?
Answer: JioFiberVoice  is powered by the JioFiber network and this will enable calls to be made over a fixed network. No need to worry if the mobile network at your home is not up to the mark. Just configure JioJoin on your mobile, and make/receive calls over the JioFiber Network.

 TV (making calls through the JioJoin app on Jio Set-top Box)

Question: How many devices can be configured with one JioFiberVoice number?
Answer:
You can configure your  JioFiberVoice  number on one landline phone and up to six additional devices such as smartphones (via the JioJoin app) or Jio Set Top Boxes. This allows you to use your JioFiberVoice number across multiple devices at home for calling and video calling. 
 At any given time, only one device can actively handle the call. This means if a call is answered on one device, the others will stop ringing. All configured devices can still make and receive calls, you simply choose which device you want to use at that moment. 
 
 This setup gives your home a flexible and seamless calling experience:
 
 Use your landline phone for regular voice calls 
 Use your smartphone for calling when you’re away from the router 
 Use your Jio Set Top Box for large-screen video calls 
 
 
 Once configured, all these devices work as extensions of your single JioFiberVoice number, giving you a complete, connected home calling solution.

Question: How do I transfer my JioFiber connection to a new location if I am relocating?
Answer:
Moving to your new home? You can easily transfer your  JioFiber  connection by submitting a relocation request online by visiting the JioFiber page on our website or through the  MyJio  app.  
 Below are the steps to check JioFiber availability at your new address and raise a relocation request using your preferred channel. 
 
 
 
 Through the Jio website  (www.jio.com) 
 
 Visit www.jio.com/fiber and click ‘Sign In’. 
 Select JioFiber and log in using OTP verification. 
 Click on ‘Settings’ (top-right corner), then select ‘Service Settings’ → ‘Relocate JioFiber’. 
 Enter your new address and tap ‘Proceed’. 
 You can either enter your address manually or use ‘Use My Current Location’. 
 If JioFiber services are available at the new location, upload your Proof of Identity (POI) and Proof of Address (POA). 
 Choose your preferred installation date and time, then submit your relocation request. 
 
 
 
 Through the  MyJio  App 
 
 Log in to the MyJio app using your JioFiber number. 
 Tap on "Home" and tap on the 'Profile' option at the top right corner. 
 Tap on ‘Service Settings’, choose ‘Relocate Connection JioFiber’. 
 Enter your new address and tap ‘Proceed’. 
 You can either enter your address manually or use ‘Use My Current Location’ for automatic detection. 
 If JioFiber is available at your new location, you’ll be prompted to upload the required documents. 
 Upload your Proof of Identity (POI) and Proof of Address (POA). 
 Select your preferred date and time for installation, then submit your Relocation Request. 
 
 
 
 Note: 
 
 Once your documents are successfully validated, a Jio engineer will visit your new address at your preferred time. 
 Please carry your existing JioFiber devices and accessories and hand them over to the engineer during installation to complete the setup. 
 You can click here to track your relocation request anytime. 
 
 
 
 
 Important points to remember while uploading documents: 
 
 Upload clear images of both front and back sides of the document. 
 Accepted file format: PDF only (file size must be less than 2 MB). 
 Ensure the file is not password-protected. 
 The name on the document must match the JioFiber account holder’s name.

Question: Can I activate Call Forwarding on my JioFiberVoice number?
Answer:
Yes, you can activate Call Forwarding on your  JioFiberVoice  number so you don’t miss calls on your landline device or any connected smartphones/ Jio Set Top Boxes .  
 You can enable different types of call forwarding based on your needs. Below are all the options and the short codes to activate or deactivate them:
 
 Unconditional Call Forwarding 
 Forward every incoming call on your JioFiberVoice number to another number. 
 Activate: Dial *401* 
 Deactivate: Dial *402 
 
 Call Forwarding on No Reply (CFNR) 
 Forward calls when you don’t pick up an incoming call on any connected extension. 
 Activate: Dial *403* 
 Deactivate: Dial *404 
 
 Call Forwarding on Number Busy (CFB) 
 Forward calls when your JioFiberVoice number is busy on another call. 
 Activate: Dial *405* 
 Deactivate: Dial *406 
 
 Call Forwarding on Not Reachable (CFNRc) 
 Forward calls when your JioFiberVoice number is out of coverage or not reachable. 
 Activate: Dial *409* 
 Deactivate: Dial *410 
 Deactivate all forwarding settings at once 
 
 
 
 In case multiple call forwarding settings are done, all can be deactivated at the same time by dialling *413.

Question: How do I troubleshoot 'no dial tone or unable to make or receive calls' issues?
Answer:
If you are facing an issue while using  JioFiberVoice  services, please follow the steps below to check and resolve it.
 
 
 
 Log in to the  MyJio  app and select 'JioFiber' 
 Scroll down to the 'JioCare: Help & Support' section 
 Click 'Run Diagnostics' 
 Select 'Voice Services' 
 Click on 'Run Diagnostics' 
 
 
 
 
If no issue is found after running diagnostics, simply follow the steps below as per the device on which you are facing the issue.
 
 For a Landline phone: 
 
 
 Please make sure your landline instrument is connected to the Jio Home Gateway. 
 Check if you can hear the dial tone. If not, reconnect the phone cable (RJ11/telephone wire cable) at both ends (landline and Jio Home Gateway). 
 Ensure that the ‘Dial’ mode is set to ‘Tone’ mode and try rebooting the JioFiber Router once. 
 
 
 
 For JioJoin on Android and iOS mobile devices: 
 
 
 Make sure your mobile device is connected to JioFiber internet. 
 If unable to complete first-time setup, visit ‘Mobile Settings’ and check if ‘Private DNS’ is enabled. Please disable it. 
 If already configured, go to ‘Settings’ in the JioJoin app and check the status of the JioFiber Voice number. It should be ONLINE. 
 If it is still not working, try uninstalling and reinstalling the app or rebooting the JioFiber Router once. 
 
 
 
 For JioJoin on Jio Set Top Box: 
 
 
 Make sure your Jio Set Top Box is connected to the Jio Home Gateway. 
 If you are making a video call through your smartphone camera, ensure the JioJoin app is in the foreground, the mobile is connected to JioFiber Wi-Fi, and camera setup is complete. 
 Ensure that the 'Mic & Camera' option is enabled in the JioJoin app mobile settings. 
 If you are making a video call through JioTVCamera, try reconnecting the camera and rebooting the Jio Set Top Box and Jio Home Gateway (Router). 
 
 
 
 
To activate JioFiber Voice services, open the provided link and follow the steps. You can also watch a video to learn how to use a landline number to make and receive calls via JioFiber.

Question: How can I make my communication 'True Secure'?
Answer:
To make your communication 'True Secure', you need to ensure the following: 
 
 Use a Jio 5G number on a 5G-compatible Android/iOS phone to register for the app. 
 Disconnect your Wi-Fi and use the same Jio 5G SIM as your mobile data while using the app. 
 
 In the case of a one-to-one voice/video call, both the parties on call need to fulfil these conditions to make the call 'True Secure'. During a call, you can see the security status of both the parties required to make the call True Secure. 
 During a Secure room call, all the online members who have joined the call need to fulfil these conditions to make the call True Secure. You can see the security status of all parties required to make the call True Secure. 
 To make a message True Secure, the sender needs to be True Secure when the message is sent and the receiver needs to be True Secure when it’s received. In the message info, you can see whether a received message is True Secure or not. 
 You will get an alert inside the app if any of these conditions need fixing to enable True Secure communication. When you are ready for True Secure communication, you will see a green shield on top of your app homepage.

Question: Do I need to be connected to Jio 5G network to use JioSafe?
Answer:
JioSafe works on all smartphone networks -4G, 5G, Wifi – as well as on all operator networks, like any other OTT communications app. There is absolutely no restriction on using JioSafe.

 

However, JioSafe users benefit from enhanced security enabled by the Jio True5G network. JioSafe offers enhanced data security with 5 layers of protection when it is on a 5G smartphone and is latched on the Jio Tru e5G network.
