Question: Can I participate in Golden Ticket and Play with Friends at the same time?
Answer: No, in Tambola you can participate only in one element of the game at a time. If you have participated in ‘Play with Friends’, you must quit the game to join ‘Golden Ticket’.

Question: If I quit Play with Friends from an ongoing game, will I get my Engage Points back?
Answer: No, once you have joined the room using your coins and you quit the Game, you won’t get your Engage Points back.

Question: How can I change the application language of JioSphere?
Answer:
JioSphere is the web browser app by Reliance Jio which lets you change the language for better browsing experience. You can do so by the following steps.

 
 Go to the hamburger menu 
 Tap on 'Settings' and then, select the 'Preference' option 
 Click on 'Select app language' and choose the preferred language 
 
 
 With that, you can set the language for the JioSphere app.

Question: How can I open multiple tabs in JioSphere?
Answer: Tap on the tab icon located at the bottom bar. After that, tap on the '+' icon located at the left bottom of the screen and a new tab will open.

Question: How can I check the version of JioSphere?
Answer: Tap on the hamburger menu and then, select 'Settings'. Over there, in the ‘About’ section, you will find the ‘App version’ details of JioSphere.

Question: How can I update the app to the new version of JioSphere?
Answer:
Your app store (Play Store) will usually indicate whether or not JioSphere is out of date and prompt you to update it. To update the app from Play Store, perform the following steps:

 
 Open Google Play Store on your device and tap on the menu at the top-left corner of the screen. 
 Tap on 'My apps & games' to see your list of installed apps. Your list will tell you which apps need to be updated. 
 JioSphere for Android will appear in the 'Updates' list. If there is an update available, tap on it. 
 Tap on the ‘Update’ button.

Question: How can I share pages from JioSphere?
Answer:
JioSphere allows you to share browsed content via social media channels, email clients as well as native device clients. Perform the following steps to share a page from JioSphere.

 
 Open the page you want to share, tap on the hamburger menu and then, select the 'Share' option in your browser 
 You may choose any of the listed options of channels – email, social media or messaging apps - to complete the sharing process

Question: How can I add a web page to the Bookmark section in JioSphere?
Answer:
Go to the web page you want to bookmark, tap on the hamburger menu and then, select the 'Add to Bookmark’ option on your JioSphere browser 
 Here you may edit the name of the bookmarked page on the browser and after that, tap on the ‘Add’ button to save it in the Bookmarks section 
 To view the bookmarked web pages in JioSphere, go to the hamburger menu and tap on Bookmarks. All the bookmarked links would be seen listed there

Question: How can I search for content on a web page in JioSphere?
Answer:
Go to the hamburger menu and then, tap on the 'Find in page' option in your browser 
 Enter the text you want to search in the text box, the page will scroll up to the section where the content is and it will be highlighted too

Question: What is Incognito Browsing and how can I enable it in JioSphere?
Answer:
Incognito Browsing supports the following:

 
 Prevents history, passwords and entries from being saved 
 Blocks cookies, temporary internet files and third-party tracking elements on web pages you visit 
 Allows you to browse in Incognito mode so that others using your phone can't see the pages you visit 
 
 
 To enable the Incognito mode, just tap on the hamburger menu, select the 'Incognito' icon and it’s done.

Question: How can I clear my browsing history of JioSphere?
Answer:
Go to the hamburger menu 
 Tap on History 
 Tap on 'Clear all history' 
 
 
 With that, your browsing history gets cleared.

Question: How can I clear the cache, cookies and web storage data of JioSphere?

Question: How can I resolve issues related to microphone in JioMeet?
Answer:
If you are experiencing issues using your microphone, follow these steps to troubleshoot the issue:

 
 Ensure the microphone is not on mute (If you see the muted Audio icon in the below-left panel, tap it to unmute yourself.) 
 If you are still muted, the host may have muted you upon entering the meeting (Ask to be unmuted by sending a chat message to the host.) 
 Try using earphones with a microphone 
 Ensure JioMeet has access to your device's microphone 
 
 
 
 
 For iOS: Go to ios Settings > Privacy > Microphone and switch on the toggle for JioMeet. 
 For Android: Go to Settings > Apps & notifications > App permissions > Microphone and switch on the toggle for JioMeet. Please, ensure that no other applications are using the microphone at the same time. 
 For iOS: View recent apps currently running. Swipe up to close an app. 
 For Android: Tap the multi-task button (the square icon usually in the bottom-right corner) to view apps currently running. Now, swipe left or right to close an app. Finally, restart your device.

 On Desktop  - While you are connected to a meeting, go to more and change layout and turn on ‘Audio-only Mode’.

In this mode, the user continues to be in the meeting, but the microphone gets muted and the video gets stopped. To talk, the user will need to tap the 'Tap to speak' icon. 
   
 This feature can be enabled/disabled in the JioMeet mobile app,

 
 iOS : Settings >>In-Call Settings >> Safe Driving Mode. 
 Android : Settings>>Meeting Settings>>Safe Driving Mode.

Question: What are the key features of the JioEyeQ dashcam?
Answer:
JioEyeQ gives you clear recording, smart alerts, and reliable drive tracking — all in one compact dashcam. 
 Here’s what you get: 
 
 Full HD 1080p recording  so every detail is clearly captured 
 140° wide-angle lens  to cover more of the road ahead 
 In-built GPS logging  to record your route, speed, and location 
 AI People Detection  for pedestrian and two-wheeler alerts 
 Collision Detection (G-sensor)  that automatically locks impact footage 
 Loop recording,  so recording never stops 
 32 GB microSD card included , ready to use 
 
 In short, it records what matters, alerts you when needed, and keeps proof safe if something unexpected happens. So, if these features are everything you were looking to add to your car, get your JioEyeQ now.

Question: My phone loses internet during JioEyeQ setup. What should I do?
Answer:
This is completely normal. When you connect to the dashcam’s Wi-Fi, your phone may lose internet because that Wi-Fi is only for camera connection. It doesn’t provide internet access. 
 Here’s what you can do while setting up your JioEyeQ: 
 
 Keep Mobile Data turned on, or 
 Enable: 
 
 
 
 Wi-Fi Assist (on iPhone), or 
 Smart Network Switch/Dual Channel Network Acceleration (on Android) 
 
 
 This allows your phone to use mobile data while staying connected to the JioEyeQ dashcam Wi-Fi.

Question: Can I change my JioEyeQ Wi-Fi password?
Answer:
Yes, you can change your JioEyeQ Wi-Fi password, and it only takes a minute. 
 Just follow these quick and easy steps: 
 
 Connect your phone to the dashcam’s Wi-Fi through the app 
 Go to Camera & Device Settings 
 Tap Wi-Fi 
 Select Edit 
 Enter the new password. Please note that only numeric passwords are supported. 
 Tap Update 
 
 With that, the dashcam will restart automatically. After that, simply reconnect your JioEyeQ using the new password.

Question: Why can’t I fast-forward or rewind JioEyeQ videos?
Answer:
To fast-forward and rewind the JioEyeQ videos, you need to download the video on your device first. 
 Playback controls work inside the ‘Downloaded Media Gallery’ in the app. If you’re streaming directly from the SD card, it’s not supported. 
 So, download the clip first and then scrub through it just the way you want.

Question: The JioEyeQ route map is not loading in the app. How do I fix it?
Answer:
If the JioEyeQ route map isn’t showing, it’s usually because of either GPS delay or internet connectivity. 
 Here’s what to check: 
 Check-up 1: Confirm GPS lock 
 Open the video and check the bottom-left corner for location coordinates. If coordinates are missing, the GPS didn’t latch during that drive. This can happen if: 
 
 You start from a basement 
 You’re in covered parking 
 There’s poor sky visibility 
 
 GPS may take a few minutes to connect. 
   Check-up 2: Ensure internet access 
 When connected to JioEyeQ’s Wi-Fi, your phone may lose internet and without internet, the map won’t load. 
 To fix this: 
 
 Turn on Wi-Fi Assist (iPhone), or 
 Enable Switch to Mobile Data/Smart Network Switch (Android) 
 
 You can also download the video and view the route from the downloaded folder in the gallery.

Question: What is AI People Detection in JioEyeQ?
Answer:
AI People Detection alerts you if the JioEyeQ dashcam detects pedestrians or two-wheeler riders in front of your vehicle. 
 It’s especially helpful in: 
 
 Busy city roads 
 Traffic signals 
 Narrow lanes 
 
 You can turn it on or off anytime from the ‘Settings’ by selecting ‘AI People Alert’. It serves as an extra layer of awareness if you need.

Question: What is Collision Detection in JioEyeQ?
Answer:
JioEyeQ’s Collision Detection feature automatically saves footage when an impact happens. 
 The built-in G-sensor detects sudden shocks or crashes and locks that clip under ‘Incident Clips’, so it doesn’t get overwritten.  
 For better accuracy, set the sensitivity of your JioEyeQ to ‘High’. That way, important moments are always protected.

Question: Does JioEyeQ record while parked?
Answer:
Yes, JioEyeQ records while parked when you install it using a hardwire kit. 
 When connected through a hardwire kit instead of the car accessory socket, the dashcam stays powered even after you switch off the engine. This allows it to record incidents while your vehicle is parked. 
 With a hardwire kit: 
 
 The dashcam remains on even when the engine is off. 
 It records bumps, impacts, or unexpected movement around your car. 
 
 So, whether it’s a minor scratch in a parking lot or sudden movement near your vehicle, your car stays monitored even when you’re away.

Question: What SD cards should I use with JioEyeQ?
Answer:
For smooth and reliable recording on JioEyeQ, you can use the following SD cards with JioEyeQ: 
 
 Class 10 or higher microSD card 
 32 GB to 128 GB capacity 
 FAT32 format 
 
  A 32 GB microSD card is included in the box, so you can start recording right away. If you drive frequently, take long highway trips, or want to store more footage before it overwrites automatically, you can upgrade to a 64 GB or 128 GB card. 
 Using the right SD card helps prevent recording errors and ensures your footage is saved clearly when you need it most.

Question: How long will my JioEyeQ SD card last?
Answer:
With around 2 hours of daily driving, a 32 GB card typically lasts 10 to 12 months. However, this can vary depending on: 
 
 Usage hours 
 Recording quality 
 Weather conditions 
 
  We recommend replacing the SD card once a year. It is a good practice for consistent performance.

Question: What is the Safety Shield feature?
Answer:
Safety Shield  is a parental control feature available on Jio feature phones 
      (JioBharat and JioPhone Prima 2). It helps parents keep their children safe 
      in the digital world and is managed remotely through the JioThings app.
    
   
 
 
 
 Restricted Numbers: 
        Only selected contacts can call children, and children can call only those contacts.
       
 
 Distraction Free: 
        Control which apps children can access to reduce distractions.
       
 
 Location Monitoring: 
        Track the child's location and view location history.

Question: How does the Safety Shield feature work on Jio devices?
Answer:
The Safety Shield feature works by connecting your JioBharat or JioPhone Prima 2 device 
  with your smartphone using the JioThings app. You need supported JioBharat models 
  (V3, V4, A1, B2) or JioPhone Prima 2, along with a smartphone running Android or iOS.
 
 
  After downloading the JioThings app, you can pair the Jio device with your smartphone. 
  Once paired, you can restrict numbers, control applications to create a distraction-free 
  environment, monitor location, and access all Safety Shield controls directly from the app.
  The JioBharat or Prima device acts as the companion device, while your smartphone acts 
  as the monitoring device.
 
 
  Both devices must stay connected to a mobile network for the Safety Shield features 
  to function smoothly.

Question: How to pair your JioBharat device with a smartphone on the JioThings App?
Answer:
For a complete step-by-step guide on how to pair a JioBharat device with a smartphone on JioThings, refer to the  User Manual . You may also follow the quick steps below. 
 
 Required for set-up 
 
 Buy a JioBharat Device. 
 Activate with an existing or new Jio SIM with a valid recharge starting at just ₹123. 
 Quickly install JioThings on your smartphone. 
 
 On the JioBharat device: 
 
 Open the JioCompanion app from the main menu. 
 Review and accept the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy. 
 Register the device using the JioBharat phone number (auto-filled). 
 Tap Yes to confirm. 
 Your device is now ready to pair. 
 
 On the smartphone: 
 
 Install and open the JioThings app from the Play Store. 
 Tap Add New, then select Phones → JioBharat. 
 Enter the JioBharat phone number to be paired. 
 Name the device for easy identification (e.g., Son, Daughter). 
 Tap Send Request to Pair, then OK to confirm. 
 
 OTP verification and setup 
 
 OTP Verification: An OTP will be sent via SMS to your JioBharat device. 
 Enter OTP: Read the OTP on your JioBharat phone and enter it in the JioThings app on your smartphone. Tap Verify PIN. 
 Connection Confirmation: Once verified, both devices will display “Phone Connected” or “Link Successful.” 
 Continue on JioBharat: On your JioBharat device, tap Continue. 
 Set Security PIN: Create a 4-digit PIN, tap Proceed, then re-enter the PIN to confirm. A pop-up will confirm successful PIN setup. 
 Applications Whitelisting: Select Application Option on JioBharat, then select the Application to be whitelisted. 
 Contact Whitelisting: Select Phonebook option, then select contacts to be whitelisted. 
 Access Dashboard: Tap Go to Dashboard to view your connected devices.

Question: What is JioRouter AX6000 (JIDU6801 IN)?
Answer:
A JioRouter is a device that connects to the internet and creates a wireless Wi-Fi network in your home or office when connected to a working modem via ethernet cable.   
 It allows multiple devices, like phones, laptops, TVs, and smart gadgets, to access high-speed internet simultaneously. It also supports advanced features like dual-band WiFi, Jio True AI Mesh coverage, and smart home connectivity. 
 Please note that the JioRouter only supports IP over DHCP and does not support/work with IPPOE and PPOE connection setup.

Question: Does JioRouter support both 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz bands?
Answer: Yes, the JioRouter (JIDU6801 IN) offers dual-band Wi-Fi (2.4 GHz & 5 GHz) with a single SSID, ensuring seamless roaming without manually switching networks.

Question: What does AX6000 stand for in JioRouter AX6000?
Answer: AX6000 refers to the Wi-Fi protocol and speed that the router can provide. AX means that the router can provide Wi-Fi 6 technology and 6000 is the max speed (in Mbps) provided by the router (4800 Mbps in 5 GHz and 1200 Mbps in 2.4 GHz).

Question: How is a Wi-Fi JioRouter better than a Wi-Fi 5 router?
Answer:
The JioRouter uses the latest Wi-Fi 6 (802.11ax) technology, offering: 
 
  Universal Compatibility (works with all ISPs) 
  Blazing-fast speeds up to 6Gbps 
  Better range and wider coverage with Mesh support 
  Connects 100+ devices smoothly with MU-MIMO and OFDMA 
  Lower latency for gaming, 4K/8K streaming and video calls 
 Advanced security with WPA3 encryption

Question: Can I use JioRouter with my existing internet connection?
Answer: Absolutely! The JioRouter is universally compatible with all ISPs (fiber, broadband, and DSL modem) when connected via ethernet. Just plug in, follow the steps on the JioHome App to set up the router, and enjoy high-speed Wi-Fi.

Question: Does the JioRouter have an IoT mode for smart home devices (2.4 GHz band)?
Answer:
Yes, your JioRouter has a dedicated 2.4 GHz IoT mode in the JioHome app which provides a stable, interference-free connection for the following: 
 
 Alexa, Google Home and Smart Assistants 
 Smart bulbs, plugs and security cameras 
 Smart TVs, doorbells and IoT device

Question: How many devices can connect to a JioRouter at the same time?
Answer: Thanks to MU-MIMO and OFDMA, the JioRouter router supports 100+ devices simultaneously, making it perfect for smart homes, offices, and families with multiple users.

Question: How secure is JioRouter?
Answer:
Your JioRouter is built with the following security features: 
 
 WPA3 encryption protects against cyber threats. 
 Built-in firewall to block unauthorised access. 
 Guest Wi-Fi access which keeps your home network safe. 
 Parental Control to safeguard your kids from dangerous websites

Question: Does JioRouter support parental controls?
Answer:
Yes, your JioRouter (JIDU6801) router includes  parental controls , allowing you to:

Question: What is Jio True AI Mesh?
Answer: Many times, a router is unable to cover the entire home due to the big size or location issue of the router. In these cases, the JioRouter can be connected with a compatible JioExtender to create a Jio True AI Mesh solution. It removes Wi-Fi dark spots and provides seamless roaming across your home using the same SSID. Please note that you need to separately purchase a JioExtender for the same.

Question: How to install a JioRouter?
Answer:
Installing a JioRouter is quick and simple using the JioHome app. 
 
 Download the JioHome app from the Play Store or iOS app store based on your device type. 
 Log in to the JioHome app with a mobile number to which the device is registered. 
 Go to the homepage, select '+' on the top right corner of the screen and select the Wi-Fi network devices option to add a router device. 
 Scan the QR code of the JioRouter present on the 'Device Label' on the back side of the Router. 
 If unable to scan the QR code, click on 'Setup without QR code', enter the RSN and model number (in FULL BLOCK LETTER) of the device and click on 'Submit'. Please note that the RSN and model number can be found on the 'Device Label' on the backside of the JioRouter. 
 Post scanning follow the steps to install the router successfully. 
 It is suggested to update the default Wi-Fi SSID and password after installation. 
 
 JioRouter comes with a Quick Startup Guide card inside the box, refer to this in case of any issue or troubleshooting the device or call us at the toll-free number 18008969999 or email at  care@jio.com  to get assistance. You can also download the Guide from  here .

Question: What is JioFind?

Question: How does JioFind's connectivity work?

Question: Can I use JioFind with a different phone number?

Question: Can I use JioFind with SIM other than the one in the packaging box?

Question: What is the Number Sharing Plan for JioFind?

The Number Sharing Plan allows you to use the same Jio phone number for both your smartphone and the JioFind device.
Key Features:
 
 
 No Extra Cost: The JioFind device uses your existing Jio phone number and its data plan, eliminating the need for a separate plan. 
 Shared Usage: The smartphone's data is shared between the smartphone and the JioFind device. 
 This plan ensures seamless connectivity for the JioFind device without requiring an additional subscription.

Question: Does JioFind work with non-Jio numbers?
Answer: No, it works exclusively with Jio phone numbers under the number-sharing plan.

Question: Can I port my Jio number after activating JioFind?
Answer: No, porting your Jio number to another network will deactivate the device.

Question: How can I track my bag/car using the JioFind device?

Question: How to start using JioDive?

Question: What should I do to get a sharper and clear image on my JioDive Headset?
Answer:
If you are not getting a clear image, here are some steps you can take to try to fix the issue:

 
 Use the IPD setting (centre wheel) to adjust the distance between the lenses to remove the double image. 
 Adjust the focus using the diopter setting (side wheels) to improve the clarity of the image. 
 Adjust the headset position using the straps to find the position that provides the clearest image. 
 Clean the lenses for any dust, smudges, and fingerprints with a microfiber cloth.

Question: How does JioTag Air work?
Answer:
JioTag Air uses Bluetooth to connect and communicate with JioThings app on your phone. The JioThings app utilises location services on your phone to identify the location of your JioTag Air.   
 
 JioTag Air is also compatible with the Apple Find My app. Once paired with Apple Find My app JioTag Air sends out a secure Bluetooth signal that can be detected by nearby iOS devices in the Apple Find My network. These devices send the GPS location of your JioTag Air to iCloud — consequently, you can view your JioTag Air on the map in the Find My app. The whole process is anonymous and encrypted to protect your privacy.  
 
 Follow the directions of the last updated location from Find My app. Once you reach within its Bluetooth range, the JioTag Air will connect automatically to your phone, and you can click Play Sound to find and retrieve it.  
 
 Please note - Only one 

 can be used at a time i.e. If you onboard JioTag Air to Apple Find My then it will not be detected by the JioThings app and vice versa.

Question: Which app do you suggest pairing with JioTag Air?
Answer: If you are an Apple user, please use Apple Find My App. JioTag Air works best with Apple Find My app for iPhones as it is an established network with millions of users around the world, that can help track lost tags in a much more effective way. JioThings app on the other hand is new and the user base is just building up. However, it’s a cross-platform app and hence with time, as more users start using the JioThings app, tracking efficiency for lost items will also enhance.

button)

Question: What does JioExtender do?
Answer: JioExtender is a device that extends Wi-Fi coverage across your home. It always ensures that the connected mobile devices are always closer to the Wi-Fi source or connected to the best signal. JioFiber Router connects with Jio Extender to expand the Wi-Fi network and thus, eliminates Wi-Fi weak zones throughout your home.

Question: How does JioExtender solve the Wi-Fi problem in my house?
Answer:
JioExtender forms two kinds of networks based on the installation. 
 
 
 Star Network where all JioExtenders are connected directly to the JioFiber home gateway. 
 Daisy Chain where one JioExtender connects to the JioFiber home gateway and the second JioExtender connects to the first JioExtender and so on creating a chain. 
 
 
 Topology formation depends on the home architecture and the method of installation chosen. By doing so, the JioExtender forms a network of Wi-Fi devices. Thus, taking the Wi-Fi source closer to mobile users and extending coverage. 
 Typically, a single JioExtender can cover 1,200 sq. ft. to 2,000 sq. ft. homes, depending on the JioExtender model, house layout, number of walls, and other construction factors.

Question: How is JioExtender different from a traditional repeater?
Answer:
JioExtenders are intelligent devices that ensure mobile client devices are always connected with the best frequency signal and to the closest Wi-Fi access point, which is called mesh technology. They instruct mobile devices to always be in good signal coverage. 
 Whereas traditional repeater doesn’t come with any of the mesh capabilities to intelligently direct the mobile users to ensure good coverage dynamically. Every repeater needs to be separately configured. 
 JioExtender doesn't need a separate configuration. It works with Jio Router; Jio Router’s Settings apply to all JioExtender in your home!

Question: What are the Battery s Do's & Don'ts?
Answer:
Here’re the dos and don’ts for the JioFi with Li-ion battery: 
 
 Do not place the battery in high-temperature environments, such as near a fire or heater 

	(It is dangerous and may cause overheating of the battery, fire, explosion, or battery function failure.) 
 If the temperature in the surrounding environment is too high or too low, the device’s performance could slow down 

	(For stable, fast charging, we recommend charging the device at room temperature.) 
 Do not heat the battery with a soldering iron, hair dryer, or other heating tools. 
 Do not immerse the battery in water; keep it in a secure, cool and dry environment. 
 Do not reverse the battery’s positive and negative terminals during usage. 
 Do not short-circuit the battery 

	(This may seriously damage the battery and may result in fire and explosion.) 
 Do not connect the battery to an electrical outlet (230V AC), as it will damage the battery and may result in fire and explosion. 
 Do not transport or store the battery with metal objects such as hairpins, keys, ornaments, etc. 
 Do not damage the battery by hitting on it with some object, throwing it on the surface, stamping on it, or dropping the battery on the surface from a certain height. 
 Do not try to disassemble the battery 

	(This will cause serious damage to the battery and it becomes unusable.) 
 Do not dispose of the batteries with your regular household waste. 
 (This can lead to poisonous chemicals and acids leaching into land and water supplies.) 
 Do not pierce the battery with a nail or other sharp objects. 
 To prevent overheating and rapid battery drainage, you are advised not to use it near an appliance which generates a strong electromagnetic field 

	(E.g. microwave oven, hair dryer, electric shaver, etc.) 
 Use only the specified power adapters when charging the Li-ion battery. 
 Wrap waste/faulty battery terminals with insulation paper for storage and transportation. 
 Do not place the battery/device inside the vehicle standing in direct sunlight for a longer duration. 
 Stop using the battery if you observe too much heat, change in colour and odour, and battery bulge. 
 Do not put heavy weight on the battery when storing it separately. 
 (It may damage the battery and may result in fire and explosion.) 
 Avoid overnight charging or leaving the device charging unattended for a longer duration. 
 Store the battery/device at normal room temperature. 
 Do not freeze the battery/device. 
 Do not place the battery/device in direct sunlight.

If the JioTag Go is in a place with many Android phones nearby its location will be quickly updated. If it is an extremely remote location like deep in a forest, or water body, its location will be updated only if another android device comes close to it. In most instances, it can function as a good low cost and maintenence free alternative to GPS Trackers.

Question: Do other Android users need to have Bluetooth turned on to be able to relay the JioTag Go's location?
Answer: Google's Find My Network service is a core service of Android and it detects the JioTag Go using BLE scan, which is always scanning. So any Android smartphone running Andorid 9+, irrespective of Bluetooth on or off,  will automatically be able to update the JioTag Gos location if it comes into proximity.

Question: Is SIM Card needed for JioTag Go?
Answer: JioTag Go does not require SIM cards and thus there are no monthly or periodical subscriptions required.

Question: Can I use the JioTag Go to find items both indoors and outdoors?
Answer: Yes, while indoors when you are in the proximity of the tag but your not able to find it, the "Play Sound" option will make the tag beep with loud 120db sound and you can hear and find it. For outdoors, the JioTag Go will connect with android devices near it and share its updated location with you on the Google FInd My Device App

Question: Is JioTag Go compatible with all mobile phones?
Answer: JioTag Go is compatible with all Android mobile phone having Android version 9 which launched in year 2018 and above. So most Android smartphones are Android 9 and above and so are compatible. JioTag Go is not compatible with iPhones. For iPhones please use JioTag Air.

Question: Is my phone compatible with both JioTag Go and JioTag Air?
Answer: If your phone is Android it is compatible with JioTag Go. If you are using an Apple phone, you need to use JioTag Air,

Question: Can I use JioTag Go with my iPhone?
Answer: JioTag Go is only compatible with Android only and pairs with Google's Find My Device. For iPhone please use JioTag Air.

Question: Can I share my JioTag Go With my Friends and Family?
Answer: Yes you can share your JioTag Go with upto 5 other people from the Google Find My Device App.

Question: Why aren't the parental control features working on my child's device, and why does it show as offline on the JioHome app?
Answer: This issue occurs because devices can change their MAC address when reconnecting to the internet. As a result, the router may take some time to map the device's new identity to its previous one. During this period, certain features like parental controls may not function properly. Once the router successfully identifies the device as the same one it was previously, parental controls will resume. However, the rules will not apply until the device is fully recognised.

Question: Made a Wrong Recharge? Heres How to Cancel It Instant
Answer:
An exclusive guide to reversing incorrect recharges via MyJio and Jio.com.

Did you just recharge with the wrong plan? It happens to the best of us! Whether you selected the wrong data pack or tapped the wrong plan, don't panic. We understand that mistakes happen, and we've got you covered.

As a valued Jio user, you have exclusive access to a Recharge Reversal feature. This self-service option allows you to cancel a wrong transaction and initiate a refund€”but you need to act fast. Here is everything you need to know to fix it right now.

The Eligibility Checklist:

Before you try to cancel, please ensure your transaction meets these criteria. This feature is designed exclusively for genuine mistakes:

Exclusive Channels: Only recharges done directly via the MyJio App or Jio.com are eligible for cancellation and refund
Note: Recharges done via third-party apps (like GPay/PhonePe/Paytm), or POS machines (Retailer / Dealer) are not eligible
Eligible Customers: This service is applicable to Mobility Retail Preid and Postpaid customers only
Recharges Only: Covers Prepaid and Postpaid recharges, but not Postpaid bill payments
Time Limit:
For standard plans: The plan must be cancelled within 3 hours of recharge
For International Roaming or ISD Plans: The plan must be cancelled within 2 hours of recharge. However, if the plan is in "Upcoming" status, then it can be cancelled within 30 days of recharge
Exclusions: This service is not applicable to Corporate or Enterprise segments
Frequency Limits: You can use this feature once every 30 days (and a maximum of 3 times per calendar year)
Recharged on a Wrong Jio Number? If you recharged the wrong Jio number, you must log in to that specific number's account to cancel the plan
How to Cancel Your Recharge (Step-by-Step)

Time is of the essence! For most standard plans, you must initiate this process within 3 hours of the successful recharge.

Open the App: Open your MyJio App and log in
Locate the Plan: Navigate to the "Recharges & History" section and follow the steps based on your account type:
For Prepaid Users: Select Recharge History. Find the specific plan you wish to cancel and click on "View Details."
For Postpaid Users (Pre-on-Post Recharges): Select Invoice History, then tap on "Recharge Transaction History." Locate the relevant plan and click on "View Details."
Find the Cancel option: Look for the "Cancel" option on the next page.
Check for Eligibility & Troubleshooting: Tap on the "Cancel Plan" option.
If the "Cancel" option is NOT reflecting: The option may take up to 30 minutes to appear. Please refresh the app. If it still doesn't appear, log in to Jio.com to check. Remember: You must complete this within the 3-hour window.

However, if you are still within the 3-hour window and cannot see the option in the app, please immediately log in to Jio.com via a browser and attempt to initiate the cancellation from there
If you see an Error Message after clicking Cancel: This indicates that the plan is no longer eligible to be cancelled (either the time limit has passed, or usage was detected)
Select Your Refund Method: If eligible, you will be asked to choose how you want your money back:
Option A (Fastest): Instant reversal to your MyJio Store Credit wallet. This is immediate and can be used for your next correct recharge
Option B: Refund to the original payment method (UPI, Bank, or Card). Note: It may take up to 21 business days for the funds to appear in your account
Confirm & Notify: Once confirmed, you will receive a notification that the transaction is cancelled
Track Your Refund: You can track the status of the recharge later by visiting the same page and clicking "Refund Status."
Recommendation: Once the reversal is initiated, we advise you to immediately recharge with the correct plan to continue enjoying uninterrupted services.

Note:

The MyJio Store Credit is a digital wallet designed for instant use on Jio services only. It cannot be transferred back to a bank account or withdrawn as cash.

If clicking "Cancel" results in a "Request Failed" or other error, it means the plan is no longer eligible for cancellation due to system checks€”typically because the allowed cancellation window has passed or usage has already been detecte

Question: I opted for a postpaid plan in the middle of the bill cycle. How much data will I get?
Answer: Plan Entitlements (including data) are assigned on a prorated basis for the first bill cycle. From the next bill cycle onwards, you will get the benefits for the complete bill cycle. E.g., if you have taken the connection on the 15th of the month for a plan which is given 2x GB data per month and the bill cycle is the 1st of every month, then the first bill will be generated for 15 days only and you will get x GB data for 15 days.

Question: How do I make calls when abroad with Jio Prepaid Plans?
Answer:
"To call a local number in the country you are visiting: Simply dial the number as locals do, including the area code (e.g., if in the US, dial 212-XXX-XXXX for New York).

To call India or another country while abroad: Use the international format: [+] [Country Code] [Mobile/Landline Number]

Example: To call an Indian mobile number, dial +91 followed by the 10-digit mobile number (e.g., +91 98XXXXXXXX).

Important: Do not add '0' before the mobile number when using the country code."
